User request:
Show a histogram chart for marketing performance summary — display the distribution of customer_age broken out by customer segment, with overlaid transparent traces and a subplot below showing the profit distribution across all segments combined

Dataframe columns:
customer_age: int
sales: float
profit: float
segment: category
region: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.title("Marketing Performance Summary – Customer & Profit Distributions")

segments = df["segment"].unique()
colors = ["#636EFA", "#EF553B", "#00CC96", "#AB63FA"]

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    subplot_titles=(
        "Customer Age Distribution by Segment",
        "Profit Distribution (All Segments)",
    ),
    vertical_spacing=0.18,
)

for i, seg in enumerate(segments):
    seg_df = df[df["segment"] == seg]
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Histogram(
            x=seg_df["customer_age"],
            name=str(seg),
            nbinsx=20,
            opacity=0.65,
            marker=dict(color=colors[i % len(colors)]),
            legendgroup=str(seg),
            showlegend=True,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

fig.add_trace(
    go.Histogram(
        x=df["profit"],
        name="All Segments",
        nbinsx=30,
        marker=dict(color="#19D3F3", opacity=0.8),
        showlegend=True,
    ),
    row=2,
    col=1,
)

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="overlay",
    height=700,
    title_text="Marketing Performance Summary",
    title_font=dict(size=18),
    legend=dict(title="Segment", orientation="v", x=1.02, y=0.95),
    template="plotly_white",
    bargap=0.05,
)

fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Customer Age", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Count", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Profit ($)", row=2, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Count", row=2, col=1)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)
Generate a Streamlit app snippet using plotly.graph_objects only. Assume df already exists. Return code only.

User request:
Show a grouped box plot comparing the distribution of profit and sales across customer segments, broken down by region, to support operations trend analysis

Available columns:
product: category
sales: float
profit: float
segment: category
region: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.title("Profit & Sales Distribution by Segment and Region")
st.markdown("Compare profit and sales spread across customer segments, grouped by region, for operations trend analysis.")

regions = df["region"].dropna().unique()
segments = df["segment"].dropna().unique()

colors = {
    "Consumer": "#1f77b4",
    "Corporate": "#ff7f0e",
    "Home Office": "#2ca02c",
}

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=1, cols=2,
    subplot_titles=("Sales Distribution by Segment & Region", "Profit Distribution by Segment & Region"),
    shared_yaxes=False
)

shown_in_legend = set()

for seg in segments:
    seg_df = df[df["segment"] == seg]
    color = colors.get(seg, "#9467bd")
    show_legend = seg not in shown_in_legend

    fig.add_trace(
        go.Box(
            y=seg_df["sales"],
            x=seg_df["region"],
            name=seg,
            marker_color=color,
            boxmean="sd",
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=show_legend,
            boxpoints="outliers",
        ),
        row=1, col=1
    )
    shown_in_legend.add(seg)

    fig.add_trace(
        go.Box(
            y=seg_df["profit"],
            x=seg_df["region"],
            name=seg,
            marker_color=color,
            boxmean="sd",
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=False,
            boxpoints="outliers",
        ),
        row=1, col=2
    )

fig.update_layout(
    boxmode="group",
    height=550,
    legend_title_text="Segment",
    title_text="Operations Trend Analysis: Sales & Profit Distributions",
    title_x=0.5,
    plot_bgcolor="#f9f9f9",
    yaxis=dict(title="Sales ($)", gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
    yaxis2=dict(title="Profit ($)", gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
    xaxis=dict(title="Region"),
    xaxis2=dict(title="Region"),
)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

User request:
Build a scatter chart comparing monthly aggregated sales vs. profit, with each customer segment shown as a separate trace and marker size scaled by number of orders, for a monthly executive dashboard

Columns in df:
sales: float
profit: float
segment: category
region: category
month: category
order_id: object

import streamlit as st import plotly.graph_objects as go st.title("Monthly Executive Dashboard: Sales vs. Profit by Segment") # Aggregate: total sales, total profit, order count per segment + month agg = ( df.groupby(["segment", "month"]) .agg(total_sales=("sales", "sum"), total_profit=("profit", "sum"), order_count=("order_id", "count")) .reset_index() ) segment_colors = { "Consumer": "#1f77b4", "Corporate": "#ff7f0e", "Home Office": "#2ca02c", } fig = go.Figure() for seg in agg["segment"].unique(): seg_df = agg[agg["segment"] == seg] fig.add_trace( go.Scatter( x=seg_df["total_sales"], y=seg_df["total_profit"], mode="markers", name=seg, marker=dict( size=seg_df["order_count"], sizemode="area", sizeref=2.0 * agg["order_count"].max() / (40.0 ** 2), sizemin=4, color=segment_colors.get(seg, "#7f7f7f"), opacity=0.75, line=dict(width=1, color="white"), ), text=seg_df["month"], hovertemplate=( "%{text}
" "Segment: " + seg + "
" "Sales: $%{x:,.0f}
" "Profit: $%{y:,.0f}
" "Orders: %{marker.size}" ), ) ) fig.update_layout( title=dict( text="Monthly Sales vs. Profit by Customer Segment", font=dict(size=20), ), xaxis=dict( title="Total Monthly Sales ($)", tickformat="$,.0f", showgrid=True, gridcolor="#e0e0e0", zeroline=False, ), yaxis=dict( title="Total Monthly Profit ($)", tickformat="$,.0f", showgrid=True, gridcolor="#e0e0e0", zeroline=True, zerolinecolor="#aaaaaa", zerolinewidth=1.5, ), legend=dict( title="Segment", bordercolor="#cccccc", borderwidth=1, ), plot_bgcolor="white", height=560, ) st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

User request:
Create a stacked bar chart showing the composition of total sales by customer segment (Consumer, Corporate, Home Office) across regions, broken down by year. Each bar should represent a region on the x-axis, with segments stacked to show how each contributes to that region's total sales. Aggregate sales per region-segment-year combination, and add one trace per segment per year so the viewer can compare both segment mix and year-over-year growth by region.

Columns in df:
region: category
sales: float
profit: float
segment: category
year: int

import streamlit as st import plotly.graph_objects as go st.title("Regional Sales Composition by Segment & Year") # Aggregate total sales by region, segment, and year agg = ( df.groupby(["region", "segment", "year"], observed=True)["sales"] .sum() .reset_index() ) regions = sorted(agg["region"].unique()) segments = sorted(agg["segment"].unique()) years = sorted(agg["year"].unique()) # Color palette: one hue family per segment, darker shade for earlier years segment_base_colors = { segments[0]: ["#1f77b4", "#aec7e8", "#6baed6"], segments[1]: ["#2ca02c", "#98df8a", "#74c476"], segments[2]: ["#d62728", "#f7b6b6", "#fc8d59"], } fig = go.Figure() for seg_idx, seg in enumerate(segments): for yr_idx, yr in enumerate(years): subset = agg[(agg["segment"] == seg) & (agg["year"] == yr)] # Align to full region list, fill missing with 0 sales_by_region = [ subset.loc[subset["region"] == r, "sales"].values[0] if r in subset["region"].values else 0 for r in regions ] color_list = segment_base_colors.get(seg, ["#7f7f7f", "#c7c7c7", "#bcbd22"]) color = color_list[yr_idx % len(color_list)] fig.add_trace( go.Bar( name=f"{seg} – {yr}", x=regions, y=sales_by_region, marker=dict( color=color, line=dict(color="white", width=0.6), ), hovertemplate=( "Region: %{x}
" "Segment: " + seg + "
" "Year: " + str(yr) + "
" "Sales: $%{y:,.0f}" ), ) ) fig.update_layout( barmode="stack", title=dict( text="Total Sales by Region — Segment & Year Composition", font=dict(size=18), ), xaxis=dict( title="Region", tickfont=dict(size=12), ), yaxis=dict( title="Total Sales ($)", tickformat="$,.0f", gridcolor="rgba(200,200,200,0.4)", ), legend=dict( title="Segment – Year", orientation="v", x=1.02, y=1, bgcolor="rgba(255,255,255,0.8)", bordercolor="lightgrey", borderwidth=1, ), plot_bgcolor="white", hovermode="x unified", height=520, ) st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

User request:
Plot a multi-line chart showing monthly revenue trends broken down by customer segment, with a separate subplot for total sales volume over the same period

Available columns:
date: datetime
revenue: float
sales: float
segment: category
region: category
year: int

import streamlit as st import plotly.graph_objects as go from plotly.subplots import make_subplots st.title("Monthly Revenue Trend by Customer Segment") # Aggregate: monthly revenue per segment df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp() monthly_segment = ( df.groupby(["month", "segment"], observed=True)["revenue"] .sum() .reset_index() ) # Aggregate: monthly total sales volume monthly_sales = ( df.groupby("month")["sales"] .sum() .reset_index() ) colors = ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd"] fig = make_subplots( rows=2, cols=1, shared_xaxes=True, vertical_spacing=0.12, subplot_titles=( "Monthly Revenue by Customer Segment", "Total Monthly Sales Volume", ), ) for i, (seg, grp) in enumerate(monthly_segment.groupby("segment", observed=True)): grp = grp.sort_values("month") fig.add_trace( go.Scatter( x=grp["month"], y=grp["revenue"], mode="lines+markers", name=str(seg), line=dict(color=colors[i % len(colors)], width=2), marker=dict(size=5), hovertemplate="%{x|%b %Y}
Revenue: $%{y:,.0f}" + str(seg) + "", ), row=1, col=1, ) monthly_sales = monthly_sales.sort_values("month") fig.add_trace( go.Scatter( x=monthly_sales["month"], y=monthly_sales["sales"], mode="lines+markers", name="Total Sales", line=dict(color="#17becf", width=2, dash="dot"), marker=dict(size=5, symbol="diamond"), fill="tozeroy", fillcolor="rgba(23,190,207,0.15)", hovertemplate="%{x|%b %Y}
Sales: $%{y:,.0f}Total Sales", showlegend=True, ), row=2, col=1, ) fig.update_layout( height=650, hovermode="x unified", legend=dict(title="Segment", orientation="v", x=1.02, y=1), plot_bgcolor="white", paper_bgcolor="white", font=dict(size=13), margin=dict(l=60, r=150, t=60, b=50), ) fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#ececec", row=1, col=1) fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#ececec", title_text="Month", row=2, col=1) fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Revenue ($)", showgrid=True, gridcolor="#ececec", row=1, col=1) fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Sales ($)", showgrid=True, gridcolor="#ececec", row=2, col=1) st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

User request:
Create a grouped bar chart with a secondary y-axis for a retail performance dashboard, showing total sales (left axis) and total units sold (right axis) broken down by region and customer segment. Aggregate both metrics per region-segment combination and display each segment as its own grouped trace pair.

Available columns:
region: category
segment: category
sales: float
profit: float
units: int

import streamlit as st import plotly.graph_objects as go from plotly.subplots import make_subplots st.title("Retail Performance Dashboard: Sales & Units by Region and Segment") # Aggregate sales and units by region and segment agg = df.groupby(["region", "segment"], observed=True).agg( total_sales=("sales", "sum"), total_units=("units", "sum") ).reset_index() segments = agg["segment"].unique().tolist() regions = agg["region"].unique().tolist() colors_sales = ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c"] colors_units = ["#aec7e8", "#ffbb78", "#98df8a"] fig = make_subplots(specs=[[{"secondary_y": True}]]) for i, seg in enumerate(segments): seg_df = agg[agg["segment"] == seg] fig.add_trace( go.Bar( x=seg_df["region"], y=seg_df["total_sales"], name=f"{seg} — Sales", offsetgroup=i, marker_color=colors_sales[i % len(colors_sales)], hovertemplate="Region: %{x}
Sales: $%{y:,.0f}" ), secondary_y=False ) fig.add_trace( go.Bar( x=seg_df["region"], y=seg_df["total_units"], name=f"{seg} — Units", offsetgroup=i, base=0, marker_color=colors_units[i % len(colors_units)], marker_pattern_shape="/", hovertemplate="Region: %{x}
Units: %{y:,}", visible="legendonly" ), secondary_y=True ) fig.update_layout( barmode="group", title_text="Total Sales & Units Sold by Region and Segment", legend_title_text="Segment / Metric", hovermode="x unified", plot_bgcolor="white", xaxis=dict(title="Region", tickangle=-30), legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, xanchor="right", x=1) ) fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Total Sales ($)", secondary_y=False, tickprefix="$", tickformat=",.0f") fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Total Units Sold", secondary_y=True, tickformat=",") st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

User request:
Create a two-panel subplot figure for financial KPI exploration: the top panel shows monthly total sales as a grouped bar chart broken out by segment, and the bottom panel shows the monthly profit margin (profit / sales) as a line chart per segment — both panels share the same x-axis (month).

Dataframe columns:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Monthly Sales & Profit Margin by Segment")

# Derive year-month period for grouping
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").astype(str)

# Aggregate sales and profit by month and segment
agg = (
    df.groupby(["month", "segment"], observed=True)
    .agg(total_sales=("sales", "sum"), total_profit=("profit", "sum"))
    .reset_index()
)
agg["profit_margin"] = agg["total_profit"] / agg["total_sales"]

segments = agg["segment"].unique().tolist()
months = agg["month"].unique().tolist()

# Color palette per segment
palette = ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd"]
color_map = {seg: palette[i % len(palette)] for i, seg in enumerate(segments)}

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.12,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Total Sales by Segment", "Monthly Profit Margin by Segment"),
)

# --- Top panel: grouped bar chart of total sales per segment ---
for seg in segments:
    seg_df = agg[agg["segment"] == seg]
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_df["month"],

User request:
Create a two-panel subplot figure for financial KPI exploration: the top panel should show monthly total sales as a line chart with markers, broken out by segment (with one trace per segment), and the bottom panel should show a grouped bar chart of total profit by region and segment. Both panels should share a clean layout with visible axis labels and a descriptive title.

Available columns:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Financial KPI Explorer: Sales Trend & Profit by Region")

# --- Prepare data ---
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()

# Monthly sales by segment
monthly_sales = (
    df.groupby(["month", "segment"], observed=True)["sales"]
    .sum()
    .reset_index()
)

# Profit by region and segment
profit_region = (
    df.groupby(["region", "segment"], observed=True)["profit"]
    .sum()
    .reset_index()
)

segments = df["segment"].unique()
regions = df["region"].unique()

colors = ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd"]

# --- Build subplot figure ---
fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=False,
    vertical_spacing=0.18,
    subplot_titles=(
        "Monthly Sales by Segment",
        "Total Profit by Region and Segment",
    ),
)

# Top panel: line chart — one trace per segment
for i, seg in enumerate(segments):
    seg_data = monthly_sales[monthly_sales["segment"] == seg]
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_data["month"],
            y=seg_data["sales"],
            mode="lines+markers",
            name=str(seg),
            line=dict(color=colors[i % len(colors)], width=2),
            marker=dict(size=5),
            legendgroup=str(seg),
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

# Bottom panel: grouped bar chart — one trace per segment
for i, seg in enumerate(segments):
    seg_data = profit_region[profit_region["segment"] == seg]
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_data["region"],
            y=seg_data["profit"],
            name=str(seg),
            marker_color=colors[i % len(colors)],
            legendgroup=str(seg),
            showlegend=False,
        ),
        row=2,
        col=1,
    )

fig.update_layout(
    height=700,
    barmode="group",
    legend=dict(title="Segment", orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.04, xanchor="right", x=1),
    title_text="Financial KPI Dashboard — Sales Trend & Profit Breakdown",
    title_font=dict(size=16),
    plot_bgcolor="white",
    paper_bgcolor="white",
)

fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Sales ($)", row=1, col=1, showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eeeeee")
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Profit ($)", row=2, col=1, showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eeeeee")

Create a subplot dashboard with two related financial KPI charts: (1) a multi-line chart showing monthly aggregated sales and profit trends over time, and (2) a grouped bar chart comparing total sales and profit by region. date: datetime

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Financial KPI Explorer")

# --- Aggregation 1: Monthly sales & profit trend ---
df["month"] = pd.to_datetime(df["date"]).dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()
monthly = df.groupby("month")[["sales", "profit"]].sum().reset_index()

# --- Aggregation 2: Sales & profit by region ---
region_summary = df.groupby("region")[["sales", "profit"]].sum().reset_index()

# --- Build subplot figure: 1 row, 2 cols ---
fig = make_subplots(
    rows=1,
    cols=2,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Sales & Profit Trend", "Sales & Profit by Region"),
    column_widths=[0.58, 0.42],
    horizontal_spacing=0.12
)

# --- Subplot 1: Multi-line trend ---
fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["sales"],
        mode="lines+markers",
        name="Sales",
        line=dict(color="#1f77b4", width=2),
        marker=dict(size=5)
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)
fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["profit"],
        mode="lines+markers",
        name="Profit",
        line=dict(color="#2ca02c", width=2, dash="dash"),
        marker=dict(size=5)
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

# --- Subplot 2: Grouped bar by region ---
fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=region_summary["region"],
        y=region_summary["sales"],
        name="Sales",
        marker_color="#1f77b4",
        showlegend=False
    ),
    row=1, col=2
)
fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=region_summary["region"],
        y=region_summary["profit"],
        name="Profit",
        marker_color="#2ca02c",
        showlegend=False
    ),
    row=1, col=2
)

# --- Layout updates ---
fig.update_layout(
    title=dict(
        text="Financial KPI Dashboard: Sales & Profit Overview",
        font=dict(size=18)
    ),
    barmode="group",
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.04, xanchor="left", x=0),
    plot_bgcolor="#f9f9f9",
    height=460
)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=1, col=1, tickangle=-30)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Amount ($)", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Region", row=1, col=2)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Amount ($)", row=1, col=2)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

User request:
Create a subplot figure with two stacked panels for financial KPI exploration: the top panel should show monthly total sales as a bar chart with one colored bar per customer segment, and the bottom panel should show monthly profit margin (profit / sales) as a line chart per segment — both panels sharing the same x-axis (month)

Available columns:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Monthly Sales & Profit Margin by Segment")

# Derive year-month period for grouping
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").astype(str)

# Aggregate sales and profit by month and segment
monthly = (
    df.groupby(["month", "segment"], observed=True)
    .agg(total_sales=("sales", "sum"), total_profit=("profit", "sum"))
    .reset_index()
)
monthly["profit_margin"] = monthly["total_profit"] / monthly["total_sales"]

segments = monthly["segment"].unique()
months = sorted(monthly["month"].unique())

colors = ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd"]

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.08,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Total Sales by Segment", "Monthly Profit Margin by Segment"),
)

for i, seg in enumerate(segments):
    seg_df = monthly[monthly["segment"] == seg].sort_values("month")
    color = colors[i % len(colors)]

    # Top panel: stacked bar for total sales
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["total_sales"],
            name=seg,
            marker_color=color,
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=True,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

    # Bottom panel: line chart for profit margin
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["profit_margin"],
            name=seg,
            mode="lines+markers",
            line=dict(color=color, width=2),
            marker=dict(size=5),
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=False,
        ),
        row=2,
        col=1,
    )

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="stack",
    height=650,
    legend=dict(title="Segment", orientation="v", x=1.02, y=1),
    plot_bgcolor="white",
    title_text="Financial KPI Explorer — Sales & Margin",
    title_x=0.5,
)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Total Sales ($)", row=1, col=1, showgrid=True, gridcolor="#ebebeb")
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Profit Margin", tickformat=".1%", row=2, col=1, showgrid=True, gridcolor="#ebebeb")
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=2, col=1, tickangle=-45)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

User request:
Build a two-panel subplot figure for financial KPI exploration: the top panel should show monthly total sales as a bar chart with one bar series per segment (Consumer, Corporate, Home Office), and the bottom panel should show monthly profit margin (profit / sales) as a line chart, also broken out by segment. Both panels should share the x-axis (month). Use aggregation over the date column to derive month-level data.

Columns in df:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Monthly Sales & Profit Margin by Segment")

# Derive year-month period for aggregation
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").astype(str)

# Aggregate sales and profit by month and segment
agg = (
    df.groupby(["month", "segment"], observed=True)
    .agg(total_sales=("sales", "sum"), total_profit=("profit", "sum"))
    .reset_index()
)
agg["profit_margin"] = agg["total_profit"] / agg["total_sales"]

segments = agg["segment"].unique()
months = sorted(agg["month"].unique())

color_map = {
    segments[i]: c
    for i, c in enumerate(["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c"])
    if i < len(segments)
}

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.10,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Total Sales by Segment", "Monthly Profit Margin by Segment"),
    row_heights=[0.55, 0.45],
)

for seg in segments:
    seg_data = agg[agg["segment"] == seg].set_index("month").reindex(months).reset_index()
    color = color_map.get(seg, "gray")

    # Top panel: grouped bar chart of total sales
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_data["month"],
            y=seg_data["total_sales"],
            name=str(seg),
            marker_color=color,
            legendgroup=str(seg),
            showlegend=True,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

    # Bottom panel: line chart of profit margin
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_data["month"],
            y=seg_data["profit_margin"],
            name=str(seg),
            mode="lines+markers",
            line=dict(color=color, width=2),
            marker=dict(size=5),
            legendgroup=str(seg),
            showlegend=False,
        ),
        row=2,
        col=1,
    )

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="group",
    height=650,
    legend=dict(title="Segment", orientation="h", y=-0.12, x=0.5, xanchor="center"),
    title_text="Financial KPI Explorer — Sales & Margin Trends",
    title_font_size=16,
    plot_bgcolor="#f9f9f9",
    paper_bgcolor="white",
)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Total Sales ($)", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Profit Margin", tickformat=".1%", row=2, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", tickangle=45, row=2, col=1)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

Create a two-panel subplot figure for financial KPI exploration — the top panel should show monthly aggregated sales as a bar chart with one trace per segment, and the bottom panel should show the monthly profit margin (profit / sales) as a line chart per segment. Share the x-axis between panels.

Dataframe columns:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Financial KPI Explorer: Monthly Sales & Profit Margin by Segment")

# Aggregate by month and segment
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()
agg = df.groupby(["month", "segment"], observed=True).agg(
    total_sales=("sales", "sum"),
    total_profit=("profit", "sum")
).reset_index()
agg["profit_margin"] = agg["total_profit"] / agg["total_sales"]

segments = agg["segment"].unique()
colors = ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd"]

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.12,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Sales by Segment", "Monthly Profit Margin by Segment")
)

for i, seg in enumerate(segments):
    seg_df = agg[agg["segment"] == seg].sort_values("month")
    color = colors[i % len(colors)]

    # Top panel: bar chart for total sales
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["total_sales"],
            name=str(seg),
            marker_color=color,
            legendgroup=str(seg),
            showlegend=True
        ),
        row=1, col=1
    )

    # Bottom panel: line chart for profit margin
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["profit_margin"],
            name=str(seg),
            mode="lines+markers",
            line=dict(color=color, width=2),
            marker=dict(size=5),
            legendgroup=str(seg),
            showlegend=False
        ),
        row=2, col=1
    )

fig.update_layout(
    height=650,
    barmode="group",
    title_text="Financial KPI Dashboard",
    legend_title_text="Segment",
    hovermode="x unified"
)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Total Sales ($)", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Profit Margin", tickformat=".1%", row=2, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=2, col=1)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Financial KPI Explorer: Sales & Profit Margin")

# Aggregate monthly totals
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()
monthly = df.groupby("month").agg(total_sales=("sales", "sum"), total_profit=("profit", "sum")).reset_index()
monthly["cumulative_sales"] = monthly["total_sales"].cumsum()
monthly["profit_margin"] = monthly["total_profit"] / monthly["total_sales"]

# Aggregate monthly profit margin by segment
monthly_seg = df.groupby(["month", "segment"]).agg(seg_sales=("sales", "sum"), seg_profit=("profit", "sum")).reset_index()
monthly_seg["seg_margin"] = monthly_seg["seg_profit"] / monthly_seg["seg_sales"]

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.12,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Total Sales with Cumulative Overlay", "Monthly Profit Margin by Segment")
)

# --- Top panel: Bar for monthly sales + Line for cumulative sales ---
fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["total_sales"],
        name="Monthly Sales",
        marker_color="steelblue",
        opacity=0.75
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["cumulative_sales"],
        name="Cumulative Sales",
        mode="lines+markers",
        line=dict(color="darkorange", width=2),
        marker=dict(size=5)
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

# --- Bottom panel: Profit margin line per segment ---
for seg in monthly_seg["segment"].unique():
    seg_data = monthly_seg[monthly_seg["segment"] == seg]
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_data["month"],
            y=seg_data["seg_margin"],
            name=f"{seg} Margin",
            mode="lines+markers",
            marker=dict(size=5)
        ),
        row=2, col=1
    )

fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Sales ($)", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Profit Margin", tickformat=".1%", row=2, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=2, col=1)
fig.update_layout(
    height=650,
    title_text="Financial KPI Dashboard — Sales & Margin Trends",
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=-0.25, xanchor="center", x=0.5),
    hovermode="x unified"
)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import plotly.graph_objects as go

User request:
Create a subplot figure with two stacked panels sharing the x-axis: the top panel shows monthly total sales as a bar chart with a separate trace per segment (Consumer, Corporate, Home Office), and the bottom panel shows the monthly profit margin (profit / sales) as a line chart — one line per segment. Use this to explore how profitability trends relate to revenue volume across segments over time.

Available columns:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Monthly Sales & Profit Margin by Segment")

# Derive year-month period for grouping
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()

# Aggregate monthly sales and profit per segment
monthly = (
    df.groupby(["month", "segment"], observed=True)
    .agg(total_sales=("sales", "sum"), total_profit=("profit", "sum"))
    .reset_index()
)
monthly["profit_margin"] = monthly["total_profit"] / monthly["total_sales"]

segments = monthly["segment"].unique()
colors = {seg: col for seg, col in zip(segments, ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c"])}

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.08,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Total Sales by Segment", "Monthly Profit Margin by Segment"),
)

for seg in segments:
    seg_df = monthly[monthly["segment"] == seg].sort_values("month")
    color = colors.get(seg, "#333333")

    # Top panel: grouped bar chart of sales
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["total_sales"],
            name=seg,
            marker_color=color,
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=True,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

    # Bottom panel: line chart of profit margin
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["profit_margin"],
            name=seg,
            mode="lines+markers",
            line=dict(color=color, width=2),
            marker=dict(size=5),
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=False,
        ),
        row=2,
        col=1,
    )

fig.update_layout(
    height=650,
    barmode="group",
    legend=dict(title="Segment", orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, xanchor="right", x=1),
    hovermode="x unified",
    template="plotly_white",
    title_text="Financial KPI Explorer: Sales Volume vs. Profit Margin",
)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Total Sales ($)", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Profit Margin", tickformat=".1%", row=2, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=2, col=1)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

User request:
Create a subplot figure with two stacked panels for financial KPI exploration: the top panel should show monthly aggregated sales as a bar chart with one trace per segment, and the bottom panel should show monthly profit margin (profit / sales) as a line chart per segment — both sharing the same x-axis.

Available columns:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Financial KPI Explorer: Monthly Sales & Profit Margin by Segment")

# Aggregate monthly sales and profit by segment
df["month"] = pd.to_datetime(df["date"]).dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()
monthly = (
    df.groupby(["month", "segment"], observed=True)[["sales", "profit"]]
    .sum()
    .reset_index()
)
monthly["profit_margin"] = monthly["profit"] / monthly["sales"]

segments = monthly["segment"].unique()

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.10,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Sales by Segment", "Monthly Profit Margin by Segment"),
)

colors = ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd"]

for i, seg in enumerate(segments):
    seg_df = monthly[monthly["segment"] == seg].sort_values("month")
    color = colors[i % len(colors)]

    # Top panel: grouped bar chart of monthly sales
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["sales"],
            name=str(seg),
            marker_color=color,
            legendgroup=str(seg),
            showlegend=True,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

    # Bottom panel: line chart of monthly profit margin
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["profit_margin"],
            name=str(seg),
            mode="lines+markers",
            line=dict(color=color, width=2),
            marker=dict(size=5),
            legendgroup=str(seg),
            showlegend=False,
        ),
        row=2,
        col=1,
    )

fig.update_layout(
    height=650,
    barmode="group",
    legend=dict(title="Segment", orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, xanchor="right", x=1),
    margin=dict(t=80, b=40),
)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Total Sales ($)", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Profit Margin", tickformat=".1%", row=2, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=2, col=1)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

Create a two-panel subplot figure for financial KPI exploration. The top panel should show monthly total sales as a bar chart with one bar series per segment (Consumer, Corporate, Home Office), stacked. The bottom panel should show monthly profit margin (profit / sales) as a line chart, one line per segment. Share the x-axis between both panels and add clear titles and axis labels.

Dataframe columns:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Monthly Sales & Profit Margin by Segment")

# Derive year-month period for grouping
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()

segments = df["segment"].unique()

# Aggregate monthly sales and profit per segment
monthly = (
    df.groupby(["month", "segment"], observed=True)
    .agg(total_sales=("sales", "sum"), total_profit=("profit", "sum"))
    .reset_index()
)
monthly["profit_margin"] = monthly["total_profit"] / monthly["total_sales"]

# Build subplot figure: 2 rows, shared x-axis
fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.10,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Total Sales by Segment", "Monthly Profit Margin by Segment"),
)

colors = {"Consumer": "#1f77b4", "Corporate": "#ff7f0e", "Home Office": "#2ca02c"}

for seg in segments:
    seg_data = monthly[monthly["segment"] == seg].sort_values("month")
    color = colors.get(seg, None)

    # Top panel: stacked bar for total sales
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_data["month"],
            y=seg_data["total_sales"],
            name=seg,
            marker_color=color,
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=True,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

    # Bottom panel: line chart for profit margin
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_data["month"],
            y=seg_data["profit_margin"],
            mode="lines+markers",
            name=seg,
            line=dict(color=color, width=2),
            marker=dict(size=5),
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=False,
        ),
        row=2,
        col=1,
    )

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="stack",
    height=650,
    legend_title_text="Segment",
    plot_bgcolor="white",
    paper_bgcolor="white",
    font=dict(size=12),
)

fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Sales ($)", row=1, col=1, tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Profit Margin", row=2, col=1, tickformat=".1%")
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=2, col=1)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

The bottom panel should show the profit margin (profit / sales) as a line chart over the same months. Both panels should share the x-axis. Include axis titles, a chart title, and a legend.

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Financial KPI Explorer — Monthly Sales, Profit & Margin")

# Aggregate to monthly totals
df["month"] = pd.to_datetime(df["date"]).dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()
monthly = df.groupby("month", as_index=False).agg(total_sales=("sales", "sum"), total_profit=("profit", "sum"))
monthly = monthly.sort_values("month")
monthly["profit_margin"] = monthly["total_profit"] / monthly["total_sales"]

month_labels = monthly["month"].dt.strftime("%b %Y")

# Build subplot figure: 2 rows, shared x-axis
fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2, cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.12,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Sales vs. Profit", "Profit Margin (%)"),
    row_heights=[0.6, 0.4]
)

# --- Top panel: grouped bars for Sales and Profit ---
fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=month_labels,
        y=monthly["total_sales"],
        name="Total Sales",
        marker_color="steelblue",
        offsetgroup=0
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=month_labels,
        y=monthly["total_profit"],
        name="Total Profit",
        marker_color="mediumseagreen",
        offsetgroup=1
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

# --- Bottom panel: profit margin line ---
fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=month_labels,
        y=monthly["profit_margin"] * 100,
        name="Profit Margin %",
        mode="lines+markers",
        line=dict(color="tomato", width=2),
        marker=dict(size=5)
    ),
    row=2, col=1
)

# Layout updates
fig.update_layout(
    title=dict(text="Monthly Financial KPIs — Sales, Profit & Margin", font=dict(size=18)),
    barmode="group",
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, xanchor="right", x=1),
    height=600,
    plot_bgcolor="white"
)

fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Amount ($)", row=1, col=1, gridcolor="lightgrey")
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Margin (%)", row=2, col=1, gridcolor="lightgrey", ticksuffix="%")
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=2, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(tickangle=45)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Monthly Sales & Profit Margin by Region")

# Aggregate to monthly totals per region
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()
monthly = df.groupby(["month", "region"], observed=True).agg(
    sales=("sales", "sum"),
    profit=("profit", "sum")
).reset_index()
monthly["profit_margin"] = monthly["profit"] / monthly["sales"]

regions = monthly["region"].unique()

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2, cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.10,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Total Sales by Region", "Monthly Profit Margin by Region")
)

colors = ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd"]

for i, region in enumerate(regions):
    region_df = monthly[monthly["region"] == region].sort_values("month")
    color = colors[i % len(colors)]

    # Top panel: grouped bar chart of total sales
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=region_df["month"],
            y=region_df["sales"],
            name=str(region),
            marker_color=color,
            legendgroup=str(region),
            showlegend=True
        ),
        row=1, col=1
    )

    # Bottom panel: line chart of profit margin
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=region_df["month"],
            y=region_df["profit_margin"],
            mode="lines+markers",
            name=str(region),
            line=dict(color=color, width=2),
            marker=dict(size=5),
            legendgroup=str(region),
            showlegend=False
        ),
        row=2, col=1
    )

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="group",
    height=650,
    legend_title_text="Region",
    template="plotly_white",
    title_text="Financial KPI Dashboard — Sales & Profit Margin",
    title_x=0.5
)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Total Sales ($)", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Profit Margin", tickformat=".0%", row=2, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=2, col=1)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

User request:
Create a two-panel subplot dashboard for financial KPI exploration: the top panel should show monthly total sales and profit as separate lines over time, and the bottom panel should show total profit by segment as a horizontal bar chart.

Dataframe columns:
date: datetime
sales: float

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Financial KPI Explorer: Monthly Trends & Segment Profitability")

# --- Aggregate monthly sales and profit ---
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()
monthly = df.groupby("month")[["sales", "profit"]].sum().reset_index()

# --- Aggregate profit by segment ---
segment_profit = df.groupby("segment")["profit"].sum().reset_index()
segment_profit = segment_profit.sort_values("profit", ascending=True)

# --- Build subplot figure: 2 rows, 1 col ---
fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=False,
    vertical_spacing=0.18,
    subplot_titles=(
        "Monthly Sales vs. Profit",
        "Total Profit by Customer Segment"
    )
)

# --- Row 1: Monthly Sales line ---
fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["sales"],
        mode="lines+markers",
        name="Total Sales",
        line=dict(color="royalblue", width=2),
        marker=dict(size=6)
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

# --- Row 1: Monthly Profit line ---
fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["profit"],
        mode="lines+markers",
        name="Total Profit",
        line=dict(color="darkorange", width=2, dash="dot"),
        marker=dict(size=6)
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

# --- Row 2: Profit by Segment horizontal bar ---
fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=segment_profit["profit"],
        y=segment_profit["segment"],
        orientation="h",
        name="Segment Profit",
        marker=dict(
            color=segment_profit["profit"],
            colorscale="RdYlGn",
            showscale=False
        )
    ),
    row=2, col=1
)

# --- Layout updates ---
fig.update_layout(
    height=700,
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.04, xanchor="right", x=1),
    plot_bgcolor="white",
    paper_bgcolor="white",
    font=dict(size=13)
)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=1, col=1, showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eeeeee")
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="USD", row=1, col=1, showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eeeeee")
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Profit (USD)", row=2, col=1, showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eeeeee")
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Segment", row=2, col=1)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

monthly = monthly.sort_values("month")

# --- Aggregate profit by segment ---
seg_profit = df.groupby("segment", as_index=False).agg(segment_profit=("profit", "sum"))
seg_profit = seg_profit.sort_values("segment_profit", ascending=True)

# --- Build subplot layout: 2 rows, 1 col ---
fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Sales vs. Profit Over Time", "Total Profit by Segment"),
    vertical_spacing=0.18,
    row_heights=[0.6, 0.4]
)

# --- Row 1: Monthly Sales line ---
fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["total_sales"],
        mode="lines+markers",
        name="Total Sales",
        line=dict(color="steelblue", width=2),
        marker=dict(size=5)
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

# --- Row 1: Monthly Profit line ---
fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["total_profit"],
        mode="lines+markers",
        name="Total Profit",
        line=dict(color="darkorange", width=2, dash="dot"),
        marker=dict(size=5)
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

# --- Row 2: Profit by Segment horizontal bar ---
fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=seg_profit["segment_profit"],
        y=seg_profit["segment"],
        orientation="h",
        name="Segment Profit",
        marker=dict(
            color=seg_profit["segment_profit"],
            colorscale="RdYlGn",
            showscale=False
        )
    ),
    row=2, col=1
)

# --- Layout updates ---
fig.update_layout(
    height=650,
    title_text="Financial KPI Dashboard",
    title_font=dict(size=18),
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, xanchor="right", x=1),
    plot_bgcolor="white"
)

fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=1, col=1, showgrid=True, gridcolor="lightgrey")

User request:
Build a box chart for operations trend analysis that compares the distribution of sales and profit across customer segments, broken down by region. Use one trace per segment per metric, grouped by region on the x-axis, with mean lines shown and outlier points visible.

Columns in df:
product: category
sales: float
profit: float
segment: category
region: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go

st.title("Sales & Profit Distribution by Segment and Region")

segments = df["segment"].unique()

palette_sales = ["#1f77b4", "#aec7e8", "#ffbb78"]
palette_profit = ["#d62728", "#ff9896", "#c5b0d5"]

fig = go.Figure()

for i, seg in enumerate(segments):
    mask = df["segment"] == seg
    fig.add_trace(go.Box(
        x=df.loc[mask, "region"],
        y=df.loc[mask, "sales"],
        name=f"{seg} – Sales",
        legendgroup=f"{seg}_sales",
        marker_color=palette_sales[i % len(palette_sales)],
        boxmean=True,
        boxpoints="outliers",
        jitter=0.3,
        line=dict(width=1.5),
        offsetgroup=f"{seg}_sales"
    ))

for i, seg in enumerate(segments):
    mask = df["segment"] == seg
    fig.add_trace(go.Box(
        x=df.loc[mask, "region"],
        y=df.loc[mask, "profit"],
        name=f"{seg} – Profit",
        legendgroup=f"{seg}_profit",
        marker_color=palette_profit[i % len(palette_profit)],
        boxmean=True,
        boxpoints="outliers",
        jitter=0.3,
        line=dict(width=1.5),
        offsetgroup=f"{seg}_profit",
        visible="legendonly"
    ))

fig.update_layout(
    title=dict(text="Operations Trend Analysis: Sales & Profit Distribution by Segment × Region"),
    boxmode="group",
    xaxis=dict(title="Region", categoryorder="category ascending"),
    yaxis=dict(title="Value ($)", zeroline=True, zerolinewidth=1, zerolinecolor="lightgrey"),
    legend=dict(title="Segment – Metric", orientation="v", x=1.01, y=1),
    height=580,
    template="plotly_white",
    margin=dict(l=60, r=180, t=70, b=60)
)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

User request:
Create a two-panel subplot dashboard for financial KPI exploration. The top panel should show monthly aggregated sales and profit as grouped bars per segment, and the bottom panel should show a monthly profit margin line (profit / sales) over time, with one line per segment.

Available columns:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Financial KPI Explorer: Sales, Profit & Margin by Segment")

# Aggregate monthly totals per segment
df["month"] = pd.to_datetime(df["date"]).dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()
monthly = df.groupby(["month", "segment"], observed=True)[["sales", "profit"]].sum().reset_index()
monthly["margin"] = monthly["profit"] / monthly["sales"]

segments = monthly["segment"].unique()
colors = ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd"]

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.12,
    subplot_titles=(
        "Monthly Sales & Profit by Segment",
        "Monthly Profit Margin (%) by Segment",
    ),
)

for i, seg in enumerate(segments):
    seg_df = monthly[monthly["segment"] == seg].sort_values("month")
    color = colors[i % len(colors)]

    # Top panel: grouped bars for sales
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["sales"],
            name=f"{seg} — Sales",
            marker_color=color,
            opacity=0.85,
            legendgroup=seg,
            offsetgroup=f"{seg}_sales",
            showlegend=True,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

    # Top panel: grouped bars for profit (lighter shade)
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["profit"],
            name=f"{seg} — Profit",
            marker_color=color,
            opacity=0.45,
            legendgroup=seg,
            offsetgroup=f"{seg}_profit",
            showlegend=True,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

    # Bottom panel: profit margin line per segment
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=(seg_df["margin"] * 100).round(2),
            mode="lines+markers",
            name=f"{seg} — Margin",
            line=dict(color=color, width=2),
            marker=dict(size=5),
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=False,
        ),
        row=2,
        col=1,
    )

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="group",
    height=700,
    hovermode="x unified",
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, xanchor="right", x=1),
    title_text="Financial KPI Dashboard",
    title_font_size=16,
)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="USD ($)", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Margin (%)", ticksuffix="%", row=2, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=2, col=1)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

User request:
Create a two-panel subplot figure for financial KPI exploration. The top panel should show monthly total sales and profit as grouped bars (one bar series per metric, grouped by month). The bottom panel should show the monthly profit margin (profit / sales * 100) as a line chart with markers. Use shared x-axes so both panels align by month. Add axis titles, subplot titles, and a descriptive figure title.

Available columns:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Financial KPI Explorer: Monthly Sales, Profit & Margin")

# Aggregate monthly totals
df["month"] = pd.to_datetime(df["date"]).dt.to_period("M").astype(str)
monthly = df.groupby("month", as_index=False).agg(
    sales=("sales", "sum"),
    profit=("profit", "sum")
)
monthly = monthly.sort_values("month")
monthly["profit_margin"] = (monthly["profit"] / monthly["sales"]) * 100

# Build 2-row subplot with shared x-axis
fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.12,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Sales vs. Profit", "Monthly Profit Margin (%)")
)

# Row 1 — Grouped bars: Sales
fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["sales"],
        name="Sales",
        marker_color="steelblue",
        offsetgroup=0
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

# Row 1 — Grouped bars: Profit
fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["profit"],
        name="Profit",
        marker_color="mediumseagreen",
        offsetgroup=1
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

# Row 2 — Line chart: Profit Margin
fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["profit_margin"],
        mode="lines+markers",
        name="Profit Margin %",
        line=dict(color="tomato", width=2),
        marker=dict(size=6, symbol="circle")
    ),
    row=2, col=1
)

fig.update_layout(
    height=620,
    title_text="Monthly Financial KPIs",
    title_font_size=18,
    barmode="group",
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, xanchor="right", x=1),
    plot_bgcolor="white"
)

fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Amount ($)", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Margin (%)", row=2, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=2, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(tickangle=45)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Monthly Sales & Profit KPI Explorer")

# Aggregate monthly totals by segment
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()
monthly = df.groupby(["month", "segment"], as_index=False).agg(
    sales=("sales", "sum"),
    profit=("profit", "sum")
)

segments = sorted(monthly["segment"].unique())

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.10,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Sales by Segment", "Monthly Profit by Segment")
)

colors = ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd"]

for i, seg in enumerate(segments):
    seg_df = monthly[monthly["segment"] == seg].sort_values("month")
    color = colors[i % len(colors)]

    # Top panel: line + markers for sales
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["sales"],
            mode="lines+markers",

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Monthly Sales & Profit KPI Explorer")

# Aggregate to monthly totals
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").astype(str)
monthly = df.groupby("month", as_index=False).agg(
    total_sales=("sales", "sum"),
    total_profit=("profit", "sum")
)
monthly = monthly.sort_values("month")
monthly["profit_margin"] = monthly["total_profit"] / monthly["total_sales"]

# Build 2-row subplot with shared x-axis
fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.12,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Sales vs. Profit", "Monthly Profit Margin (%)"),
    row_heights=[0.6, 0.4]
)

# Row 1 — Grouped bars: Sales
fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["total_sales"],
        name="Total Sales",
        marker_color="#1f77b4",
        offsetgroup=0
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

# Row 1 — Grouped bars: Profit
fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["total_profit"],
        name="Total Profit",
        marker_color="#2ca02c",
        offsetgroup=1
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

# Row 2 — Line: Profit Margin
fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["profit_margin"] * 100,
        mode="lines+markers",
        name="Profit Margin %",
        line=dict(color="#d62728", width=2),
        marker=dict(size=5)
    ),
    row=2, col=1
)

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="group",
    height=650,
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, xanchor="right", x=1),
    title_text="Financial KPI Overview — Sales, Profit & Margin by Month",
    title_font_size=16,
    plot_bgcolor="white"
)

fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Amount ($)", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Margin (%)", row=2, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=2, col=1, tickangle=45)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

User request:
Build a two-panel subplot dashboard for financial KPI exploration: the top panel should show monthly total sales and profit as grouped bars per segment (Consumer, Corporate, Home Office), and the bottom panel should show the monthly profit margin (profit / sales) as a line chart over time, with one line per segment. Share the x-axis between the two panels.

Columns in df:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Financial KPI Explorer: Sales, Profit & Margin by Segment")

# Derive year-month period for grouping
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()

# Aggregate monthly sales and profit by segment
monthly = (
    df.groupby(["month", "segment"], observed=True)
    .agg(sales=("sales", "sum"), profit=("profit", "sum"))
    .reset_index()
)
monthly["profit_margin"] = monthly["profit"] / monthly["sales"]

segments = monthly["segment"].unique()

# Color palette per segment
palette = {}
default_colors = ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd"]
for i, seg in enumerate(segments):
    palette[seg] = default_colors[i % len(default_colors)]

# Build 2-row subplot with shared x-axis
fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.12,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Sales & Profit by Segment", "Monthly Profit Margin by Segment"),
    row_heights=[0.55, 0.45],
)

# --- Top panel: grouped bars for sales and profit per segment ---
for seg in segments:
    seg_df = monthly[monthly["segment"] == seg]
    # Sales bar
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["sales"],
            name=f"{seg} — Sales",
            marker_color=palette[seg],
            opacity=0.85,
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=True,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )
    # Profit bar (lighter shade via opacity)
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["profit"],
            name=f"{seg} — Profit",
            marker_color=palette[seg],
            opacity=0.45,
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=True,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

# --- Bottom panel: profit margin lines per segment ---
for seg in segments:
    seg_df = monthly[monthly["segment"] == seg]
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["profit_margin"],
            mode="lines+markers",
            name=f"{seg} — Margin",
            line=dict(color=palette[seg], width=2),
            marker=dict(size=5),
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=False,
        ),
        row=2,
        col=1,
    )

# Layout updates
fig.update_layout(
    barmode="group",
    height=680,
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, xanchor="right", x=1),
    plot_bgcolor="#f9f9f9",
    paper_bgcolor="#ffffff",
    margin=dict(t=80, b=40, l=60, r=40),
)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Amount ($)", row=1, col=1, tickformat="$,.0f")
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Profit Margin", row=2, col=1, tickformat=".1%")
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=2, col=1)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.title("Sales & Profit Distribution by Segment and Region")

segments = df["segment"].unique()
regions = df["region"].unique()

color_map = {
    "East": "#1f77b4",
    "West": "#ff7f0e",
    "Central": "#2ca02c",
    "South": "#d62728",
}

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=1, cols=2,
    subplot_titles=("Sales Distribution by Segment", "Profit Distribution by Segment"),
    shared_yaxes=False,
)

for region in regions:
    region_df = df[df["region"] == region]
    color = color_map.get(str(region), "#7f7f7f")

    fig.add_trace(
        go.Box(
            x=region_df["segment"],
            y=region_df["sales"],
            name=str(region),
            marker_color=color,
            boxpoints="outliers",
            legendgroup=str(region),
            showlegend=True,
        ),
        row=1, col=1,
    )

    fig.add_trace(
        go.Box(
            x=region_df["segment"],
            y=region_df["profit"],
            name=str(region),
            marker_color=color,
            boxpoints="outliers",
            legendgroup=str(region),
            showlegend=False,
        ),
        row=1, col=2,
    )

fig.update_layout(
    boxmode="group",
    height=520,
    legend_title_text="Region",
    template="plotly_white",
    xaxis=dict(title="Segment"),
    xaxis2=dict(title="Segment"),
    yaxis=dict(title="Sales ($)"),
    yaxis2=dict(title="Profit ($)"),
)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

}

Build a two-panel subplot dashboard for financial KPI exploration: the top panel should show monthly aggregated sales and profit as grouped bars by segment, and the bottom panel should show a monthly profit margin trend line per segment.

Columns in df:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float

segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Financial KPI Explorer: Sales, Profit & Margin by Segment")

# Derive month period for aggregation
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()

# Aggregate monthly sales and profit by segment
monthly = (
    df.groupby(["month", "segment"], observed=True)
    .agg(sales=("sales", "sum"), profit=("profit", "sum"))
    .reset_index()
)
monthly["margin"] = monthly["profit"] / monthly["sales"] * 100

segments = monthly["segment"].unique()

# Color palette per segment
colors = ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd"]

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.12,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Sales & Profit by Segment", "Monthly Profit Margin (%) by Segment"),
)

for i, seg in enumerate(segments):
    seg_df = monthly[monthly["segment"] == seg].sort_values("month")
    color = colors[i % len(colors)]

    # Top panel: grouped bars for sales
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["sales"],
            name=f"{seg} Sales",
            marker_color=color,
            opacity=0.85,
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=True,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

    # Top panel: grouped bars for profit (lighter shade via opacity)
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["profit"],
            name=f"{seg} Profit",
            marker_color=color,
            opacity=0.4,
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=True,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

    # Bottom panel: profit margin trend line
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["margin"],
            mode="lines+markers",
            name=f"{seg} Margin",
            line=dict(color=color, width=2),
            marker=dict(size=6),
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=False,
        ),
        row=2,
        col=1,
    )

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="group",
    height=700,
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, xanchor="right", x=1),
    plot_bgcolor="white",
    paper_bgcolor="white",
    font=dict(size=12),
)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Amount ($)", row=1, col=1, tickformat="$,.0f", gridcolor="#eeeeee")
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Margin (%)", row=2, col=1, ticksuffix="%", gridcolor="#eeeeee")
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=2, col=1)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st import plotly.graph_objects as go st.title("Monthly Executive Dashboard – Sales vs. Profit by Segment") # Aggregate: sum sales, profit, and units per segment + month agg = ( df.groupby(["segment", "month"], observed=True) .agg(total_sales=("sales", "sum"), total_profit=("profit", "sum"), total_units=("units", "sum")) .reset_index() ) month_order = ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun", "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"] existing_months = [m for m in month_order if m in agg["month"].unique()] agg["month"] = agg["month"].astype(str) colors = {"Consumer": "#1f77b4", "Corporate": "#ff7f0e", "Home Office": "#2ca02c"} fig = go.Figure() for seg, grp in agg.groupby("segment", observed=True): grp = grp.copy() grp["month"] = grp["month"].astype("category") grp["month_sort"] = grp["month"].apply( lambda m: month_order.index(m) if m in month_order else 99 ) grp = grp.sort_values("month_sort") # Normalise marker size: scale units to range 8–40 min_u = grp["total_units"].min() max_u = grp["total_units"].max() size_range = max_u - min_u if max_u != min_u else 1 marker_sizes = 8 + 32 * (grp["total_units"] - min_u) / size_range fig.add_trace( go.Scatter( x=grp["total_sales"], y=grp["total_profit"], mode="markers+text", name=seg, text=grp["month"], textposition="top center", marker=dict( size=marker_sizes, color=colors.get(seg, "#9467bd"), opacity=0.80, line=dict(width=1, color="white"), ), hovertemplate=( "%s

User request:
Create a two-panel subplot dashboard for financial KPI exploration. The top panel should show monthly total sales as a bar chart with one bar series per segment (Consumer, Corporate, Home Office). The bottom panel should show monthly profit margin (profit / sales) as a line chart, also broken out by segment. Share the x-axis between both panels and add a descriptive title to the overall figure.

Dataframe columns:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Financial KPI Explorer — Sales & Profit Margin by Segment")

# Derive year-month period for grouping
df["year_month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").astype(str)

# Aggregate monthly sales and profit per segment
monthly = (
    df.groupby(["year_month", "segment"], observed=True)
    .agg(total_sales=("sales", "sum"), total_profit=("profit", "sum"))
    .reset_index()
)
monthly["profit_margin"] = monthly["total_profit"] / monthly["total_sales"]

segments = monthly["segment"].unique()

# Colour palette per segment
palette = {"Consumer": "#1f77b4", "Corporate": "#ff7f0e", "Home Office": "#2ca02c"}

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.10,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Sales by Segment ($)", "Monthly Profit Margin by Segment (%)"),
)

for seg in segments:
    seg_data = monthly[monthly["segment"] == seg].sort_values("year_month")
    color = palette.get(seg, None)

    # Top panel — grouped bar chart of total sales
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_data["year_month"],
            y=seg_data["total_sales"],
            name=seg,
            marker_color=color,
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=True,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

    # Bottom panel — line chart of profit margin
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_data["year_month"],
            y=seg_data["profit_margin"],
            name=seg,
            mode="lines+markers",
            line=dict(color=color, width=2),
            marker=dict(size=5),
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=False,
        ),
        row=2,
        col=1,
    )

fig.update_layout(
    title=dict(text="Monthly Sales & Profit Margin KPIs by Customer Segment", font=dict(size=18)),
    barmode="group",
    legend=dict(title="Segment", orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.04, xanchor="right", x=1),
    height=700,
    plot_bgcolor="white",
    paper_bgcolor="white",
)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Total Sales ($)", row=1, col=1, tickprefix="$", showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eeeeee")
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Profit Margin", row=2, col=1, tickformat=".1%", showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eeeeee")
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=2, col=1, tickangle=-45)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

st.title("Financial KPI Dashboard: Sales & Profit Explorer")

# --- Aggregation for top panel: monthly sales and profit margin by segment ---
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").astype(str)

monthly = (
    df.groupby(["month", "segment"], as_index=False)
    .agg(total_sales=("sales", "sum"), total_profit=("profit", "sum"))
)
monthly["profit_margin"] = monthly["total_profit"] / monthly["total_sales"] * 100

# Overall monthly totals for the margin line
monthly_total = (
    df.groupby("month", as_index=False)
    .agg(total_sales=("sales", "sum"), total_profit=("profit", "sum"))
)
monthly_total["profit_margin"] = monthly_total["total_profit"] / monthly_total["total_sales"] * 100

# --- Aggregation for bottom panel: total profit by region and segment ---
region_seg = (
    df.groupby(["region", "segment"], as_index=False)
    .agg(total_profit=("profit", "sum"))
)

segments = region_seg["segment"].unique().tolist()
regions = region_seg["region"].unique().tolist()

# --- Build subplot figure: 2 rows ---
fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    subplot_titles=(
        "Monthly Sales by Segment with Overall Profit Margin (%)",
        "Total Profit by Region and Segment",
    ),
    vertical_spacing=0.18,
    row_heights=[0.55, 0.45],
    specs=[[{"secondary_y": True}], [{"secondary_y": False}]],
)

# Top panel — stacked bar of monthly sales per segment
color_map = {
    segments[i]: clr
    for i, clr in enumerate(["#4C78A8", "#F58518", "#E45756", "#72B7B2", "#54A24B"])
    if i < len(segments)
}

for seg in segments:
    seg_data = monthly[monthly["segment"] == seg].sort_values("month")
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_data["month"],
            y=seg_data["total_sales"],
            name=seg,
            marker_color=color_map.get(seg, "#888"),
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=True,
        ),
        row=1, col=1, secondary_y=False,
    )

# Top panel — profit margin line on secondary y-axis
monthly_total_sorted = monthly_total.sort_values("month")
fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=monthly_total_sorted["month"],
        y=monthly_total_sorted["profit_margin"],
        name="Profit Margin (%)",
        mode="lines+markers",
        line=dict(color="#FF4136", width=2, dash="dot"),
        marker=dict(size=6),
        yaxis="y2",
    ),
    row=1, col=1, secondary_y=True,
)

# Bottom panel — stacked bar of total profit by region, colored by segment
bottom_colors = ["#4C78A8", "#F58518", "#E45756", "#72B7B2", "#54A24B"]
for i, seg in enumerate(segments):
    seg_data = region_seg[region_seg["segment"] == seg].sort_values("region")
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_data["region"],
            y=seg_data["total_profit"],
            name=seg,
            marker_color=bottom_colors[i % len(bottom_colors)],
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=False,
        ),
        row=2, col=1,
    )

# --- Layout updates ---
fig.update_layout(
    barmode="stack",

        text="Financial KPI Explorer: Monthly Sales & Regional Profit Breakdown",

)

fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Total Sales ($)", row=1, col=1, secondary_y=False)

Create a two-panel subplot dashboard for financial KPI exploration. The top panel should show monthly total sales as a bar chart with one bar series per segment (Consumer, Corporate, Home Office). The bottom panel should show monthly profit margin (profit / sales) as a line chart, also broken out by segment. Both panels should share the x-axis and have clear axis labels and a descriptive title.

Available columns:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Financial KPI Explorer: Sales & Profit Margin by Segment")

# Derive month period and compute monthly aggregates per segment
df["month"] = pd.to_datetime(df["date"]).dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()

monthly = (
    df.groupby(["month", "segment"], observed=True)
    .agg(total_sales=("sales", "sum"), total_profit=("profit", "sum"))
    .reset_index()
)
monthly["profit_margin"] = monthly["total_profit"] / monthly["total_sales"]

segments = monthly["segment"].unique()

color_map = {
    "Consumer": "#1f77b4",
    "Corporate": "#ff7f0e",
    "Home Office": "#2ca02c",
}

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.12,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Sales by Segment", "Monthly Profit Margin by Segment"),
)

for seg in segments:
    seg_df = monthly[monthly["segment"] == seg].sort_values("month")
    color = color_map.get(str(seg), None)

    # Top panel: grouped bar chart of total sales
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["total_sales"],
            name=str(seg),
            marker_color=color,
            legendgroup=str(seg),
            showlegend=True,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

    # Bottom panel: line chart of profit margin
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["profit_margin"],
            mode="lines+markers",
            name=str(seg),
            line=dict(color=color, width=2),
            marker=dict(size=5),
            legendgroup=str(seg),
            showlegend=False,
        ),
        row=2,
        col=1,
    )

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="group",
    height=650,
    title_text="Monthly Sales & Profit Margin KPI Dashboard",
    title_font_size=18,
    legend_title_text="Segment",
    hovermode="x unified",
    plot_bgcolor="white",
    paper_bgcolor="white",
)

fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Total Sales ($)", tickformat="$,.0f", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Profit Margin", tickformat=".1%", row=2, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=2, col=1)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

User request:
Build a two-panel subplot dashboard for financial KPI exploration: the top panel should show monthly total sales and profit as grouped bars (one trace per metric), and the bottom panel should show the profit margin percentage (profit/sales) as a line chart over time — both panels sharing the x-axis.

Columns in df:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Financial KPI Explorer: Sales, Profit & Margin Over Time")

# Aggregate by month
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()
monthly = df.groupby("month", as_index=False).agg(total_sales=("sales", "sum"), total_profit=("profit", "sum"))
monthly["profit_margin"] = (monthly["total_profit"] / monthly["total_sales"] * 100).round(2)

# Build subplot figure: 2 rows, shared x-axis
fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.12,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Sales vs. Profit", "Profit Margin (%)")
)

# Row 1 — Grouped bar: Sales
fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["total_sales"],
        name="Total Sales",
        marker_color="steelblue",
        opacity=0.85
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

# Row 1 — Grouped bar: Profit
fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["total_profit"],
        name="Total Profit",
        marker_color="mediumseagreen",
        opacity=0.85
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

# Row 2 — Line chart: Profit Margin %
fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["profit_margin"],
