User request:
Create a two-panel subplot figure for financial KPI exploration: the top panel should show monthly total sales as a bar chart with a line overlay for monthly total profit, and the bottom panel should show profit margin (profit / sales) as a line chart over time — grouped by month from the date column. Each segment should appear as a separate trace in both panels.

Available columns:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Financial KPI Explorer — Sales, Profit & Margin by Segment")

# Derive year-month period for grouping
df["month"] = pd.to_datetime(df["date"]).dt.to_period("M").astype(str)

# Aggregate by month and segment
monthly = (
    df.groupby(["month", "segment"], observed=True)
    .agg(total_sales=("sales", "sum"), total_profit=("profit", "sum"))
    .reset_index()
)
monthly["profit_margin"] = monthly["total_profit"] / monthly["total_sales"]

segments = monthly["segment"].unique()
months = sorted(monthly["month"].unique())

# Color palette per segment
palette = ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd"]
seg_color = {seg: palette[i % len(palette)] for i, seg in enumerate(segments)}

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.12,
    subplot_titles=(
        "Monthly Sales (bars) & Profit (lines) by Segment",
        "Monthly Profit Margin by Segment",
    ),
)

for seg in segments:
    seg_df = monthly[monthly["segment"] == seg].sort_values("month")
    color = seg_color[seg]

    # Top panel — Sales bars
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["total_sales"],
            name=f"{seg} — Sales",
            marker_color=color,
            opacity=0.7,
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=True,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

    # Top panel — Profit line overlay
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["total_profit"],
            name=f"{seg} — Profit",
            mode="lines+markers",
            line=dict(color=color, dash="dot", width=2),
            marker=dict(size=5),
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=True,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

    # Bottom panel — Profit margin line
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["profit_margin"],
            name=f"{seg} — Margin",
            mode="lines+markers",
            line=dict(color=color, width=2),
            marker=dict(symbol="diamond", size=6),
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=False,
        ),
        row=2,
        col=1,
    )

fig.update_layout(
    height=700,
    barmode="group",
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, xanchor="right", x=1),
    plot_bgcolor="#f9f9f9",
    title_text="Financial KPI Dashboard",
    title_font_size=18,
)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Amount ($)", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Profit Margin", tickformat=".1%", row=2, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", tickangle=45, row=2, col=1)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.title("Profit Distribution by Segment & Product — Operations Trend Analysis")

segments = df["segment"].dropna().unique()
products = df["product"].dropna().unique()

colors = {
    products[i]: ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd"][i % 5]
    for i in range(len(products))
}

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=1,
    cols=len(segments),
    subplot_titles=[str(s) for s in segments],
    shared_yaxes=True,
)

for col_idx, segment in enumerate(segments):
    seg_df = df[df["segment"] == segment]
    for product in products:
        prod_df = seg_df[seg_df["product"] == product]
        fig.add_trace(
            go.Box(
                y=prod_df["profit"],
                name=str(product),
                legendgroup=str(product),
                showlegend=(col_idx == 0),
                marker_color=colors[product],
                boxmean="sd",
                boxpoints="outliers",
                line_width=1.5,
            ),
            row=1,
            col=col_idx + 1,
        )

fig.update_layout(
    title=dict(text="Profit Distribution by Customer Segment and Product Category", x=0.5),
    boxmode="group",
    legend_title_text="Product",
    height=520,
    plot_bgcolor="#f9f9f9",
    yaxis_title="Profit ($)",
    font=dict(size=12),
)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.title("Profit & Sales Distribution by Product and Segment")
st.markdown("Compare profit and sales spread across product categories and customer segments to identify operational variability.")

segments = df["segment"].dropna().unique()
products = df["product"].dropna().unique()

colors = {
    segments[0]: "#1f77b4",
    segments[1]: "#ff7f0e",
    segments[2]: "#2ca02c",
}

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=1, cols=2,
    subplot_titles=("Profit Distribution by Product & Segment",
                    "Sales Distribution by Product & Segment"),
    shared_yaxes=False
)

shown_in_legend = set()

for seg in segments:
    seg_df = df[df["segment"] == seg]
    color = colors.get(seg, "#999999")
    show_legend = seg not in shown_in_legend

    fig.add_trace(
        go.Box(
            y=seg_df["profit"],
            x=seg_df["product"],
            name=str(seg),
            marker_color=color,
            boxmean="sd",
            legendgroup=str(seg),
            showlegend=show_legend,
            boxpoints="outliers",
        ),
        row=1, col=1
    )
    shown_in_legend.add(seg)

    fig.add_trace(
        go.Box(
            y=seg_df["sales"],
            x=seg_df["product"],
            name=str(seg),
            marker_color=color,
            boxmean="sd",
            legendgroup=str(seg),
            showlegend=False,
            boxpoints="outliers",
        ),
        row=1, col=2
    )

fig.update_layout(
    boxmode="group",
    height=550,
    legend_title_text="Segment",
    template="plotly_white",
    title=dict(
        text="Operations Trend Analysis: Profit & Sales Distributions",
        x=0.5,
        xanchor="center"
    ),
)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Profit ($)", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Sales ($)", row=1, col=2)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Product Category", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Product Category", row=1, col=2)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.title("Sales & Profit Distribution by Segment and Region")

segments = df["segment"].unique()
regions = df["region"].unique()

color_map = {
    "East": "#1f77b4",
    "West": "#ff7f0e",
    "Central": "#2ca02c",
    "South": "#d62728",
}

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=1,
    cols=2,
    subplot_titles=("Sales Distribution by Segment & Region", "Profit Distribution by Segment & Region"),
    shared_yaxes=False,
)

shown_in_legend = set()

for region in regions:
    region_df = df[df["region"] == region]
    color = color_map.get(region, "#888888")
    show_legend = region not in shown_in_legend

    fig.add_trace(
        go.Box(
            y=region_df["sales"],
            x=region_df["segment"],
            name=region,
            marker_color=color,
            boxpoints="outliers",
            legendgroup=region,
            showlegend=show_legend,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )
    shown_in_legend.add(region)

    fig.add_trace(
        go.Box(
            y=region_df["profit"],
            x=region_df["segment"],
            name=region,
            marker_color=color,
            boxpoints="outliers",
            legendgroup=region,
            showlegend=False,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=2,
    )

fig.update_layout(
    boxmode="group",
    height=560,
    legend_title_text="Region",
    title_text="Operations Trend Analysis: Sales & Profit Spread by Segment and Region",
    plot_bgcolor="#f9f9f9",
    font=dict(size=12),
)

fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Sales (USD)", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Profit (USD)", row=1, col=2)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Segment", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Segment", row=1, col=2)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.title("Operations Trend Analysis: Profit & Sales Distribution by Segment and Product")

segments = df["segment"].dropna().unique()
products = df["product"].dropna().unique()

fig.update_layout(

date: datetime

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Financial KPI Explorer: Sales, Profit & Margin by Segment")

# Aggregate monthly totals by segment
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()
monthly = df.groupby(["month", "segment"], as_index=False).agg(
    sales=("sales", "sum"),
    profit=("profit", "sum")
)
monthly["margin"] = monthly["profit"] / monthly["sales"]

segments = monthly["segment"].unique()

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.12,
    subplot_titles=(
        "Monthly Sales & Profit by Segment",
        "Profit Margin (%) Trend by Segment"
    )
)

colors_sales = ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c"]
colors_profit = ["#aec7e8", "#ffbb78", "#98df8a"]
colors_margin = ["#d62728", "#9467bd", "#8c564b"]

for i, seg in enumerate(segments):
    seg_df = monthly[monthly["segment"] == seg].sort_values("month")

    # Top panel: grouped bars for sales
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["sales"],
            name=f"{seg} Sales",
            marker_color=colors_sales[i % len(colors_sales)],
            offsetgroup=i,
            legendgroup=seg,
        ),
        row=1, col=1
    )

    # Top panel: grouped bars for profit
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["profit"],
            name=f"{seg} Profit",
            marker_color=colors_profit[i % len(colors_profit)],
            offsetgroup=i + len(segments),
            legendgroup=seg,
        ),
        row=1, col=1
    )

    # Bottom panel: line for profit margin
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["margin"],
            mode="lines+markers",
            name=f"{seg} Margin",
            line=dict(color=colors_margin[i % len(colors_margin)], width=2),
            marker=dict(size=5),
            legendgroup=seg,
        ),
        row=2, col=1
    )

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="group",
    height=650,
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=-0.25, xanchor="center", x=0.5),
    title_text="Financial KPI Dashboard — Segment Performance",
    title_font_size=16,
)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Amount ($)", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Profit Margin", tickformat=".0%", row=2, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=2, col=1)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

# Aggregate to monthly level

monthly = (

    .reset_index()
)
monthly["profit_margin"] = monthly["total_profit"] / monthly["total_sales"]

segments = monthly["segment"].unique()

colors = ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd"]

)

    # Top panel: grouped bar — total sales
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["total_sales"],

            marker_color=color,

            showlegend=True,

        row=1,
        col=1,

    # Bottom panel: line — profit margin
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["profit_margin"],
            name=str(seg),
            mode="lines+markers",
            line=dict(color=color, width=2),
            marker=dict(size=5),
            legendgroup=str(seg),

fig.update_layout(

)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Total Sales ($)", row=1, col=1, tickformat="$,.0f", showgrid=True, gridcolor="#e5e5e5")

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go

st.title("Monthly Sales, Profit & Margin KPI Dashboard")

# Aggregate to monthly totals
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()
monthly = df.groupby("month", as_index=False).agg(total_sales=("sales", "sum"), total_profit=("profit", "sum"))
monthly["profit_margin"] = (monthly["total_profit"] / monthly["total_sales"] * 100).round(2)

# Build 2-row subplot with shared x-axis
fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,

        marker_color="steelblue",
        offsetgroup=0
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

# Row 1: grouped bar — Profit
fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["total_profit"],
        name="Total Profit",
        marker_color="mediumseagreen",
        offsetgroup=1
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["profit_margin"],
        mode="lines+markers",
        name="Profit Margin (%)",
        line=dict(color="darkorange", width=2),
        marker=dict(size=5)
    ),
    row=2, col=1
)

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="group",
    height=600,
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, xanchor="right", x=1),

)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Amount ($)", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Margin (%)", row=2, col=1)

User request:
Create a stacked subplot dashboard with two panels: (1) a multi-line chart showing monthly total sales and profit trends over time by segment, and (2) a grouped bar chart comparing total sales vs profit per product — all in one figure for financial KPI exploration

Available columns:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.title("Financial KPI Explorer: Sales & Profit Trends")

# --- Prep: monthly segment trends for top panel ---
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()
monthly_seg = (
    df.groupby(["month", "segment"])[["sales", "profit"]]
    .sum()
    .reset_index()
)

# --- Prep: product-level totals for bottom panel ---
product_totals = (
    df.groupby("product")[["sales", "profit"]]
    .sum()
    .reset_index()
    .sort_values("sales", ascending=False)
)

segments = monthly_seg["segment"].unique()

# Color palette per segment
seg_colors = ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd"]

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=False,
    vertical_spacing=0.14,
    subplot_titles=(
        "Monthly Sales & Profit by Segment",
        "Total Sales vs Profit by Product",
    ),
)

# --- Top panel: one Sales line + one Profit line per segment ---
for i, seg in enumerate(segments):
    seg_df = monthly_seg[monthly_seg["segment"] == seg].sort_values("month")
    color = seg_colors[i % len(seg_colors)]

    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["sales"],
            mode="lines+markers",
            name=f"{seg} – Sales",
            line=dict(color=color, width=2),
            marker=dict(size=5),
            legendgroup=seg,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["profit"],
            mode="lines",
            name=f"{seg} – Profit",
            line=dict(color=color, width=2, dash="dot"),
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=True,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

# --- Bottom panel: grouped bars – Sales and Profit per product ---
fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=product_totals["product"],
        y=product_totals["sales"],
        name="Total Sales",
        marker_color="#1f77b4",
        offsetgroup=0,
    ),
    row=2,
    col=1,
)

fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=product_totals["product"],
        y=product_totals["profit"],
        name="Total Profit",
        marker_color="#2ca02c",
        offsetgroup=1,
    ),
    row=2,
    col=1,
)

fig.update_layout(
    height=750,
    barmode="group",
    legend=dict(orientation="v", x=1.02, y=1),
    plot_bgcolor="#f9f9f9",
    paper_bgcolor="white",
    font=dict(size=12),
    title=dict(
        text="Financial KPI Dashboard – Segment Trends & Product Performance",
        x=0.5,
        font=dict(size=15),
    ),
)

fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Amount ($)", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Amount ($)", row=2, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Product", row=2, col=1, tickangle=-30)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)
Generate a Streamlit app snippet using plotly.graph_objects only. Assume df already exists. Return code only.

User request:
Create a two-panel subplot figure for financial KPI exploration. The top panel should show monthly total sales as a bar chart with one bar series per segment (Consumer, Corporate, Home Office). The bottom panel should show monthly profit margin (profit / sales) as a line chart, one line per segment. Share the x-axis between the two panels and add a descriptive title to the full figure.

Available columns:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Financial KPI Explorer — Sales & Profit Margin by Segment")

# Derive year-month period for grouping
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()

# Aggregate monthly sales and profit per segment
monthly = (
    df.groupby(["month", "segment"], observed=True)
    .agg(total_sales=("sales", "sum"), total_profit=("profit", "sum"))
    .reset_index()
)
monthly["profit_margin"] = monthly["total_profit"] / monthly["total_sales"]

segments = monthly["segment"].unique()

colors = {
    segments[0]: "#1f77b4",
    segments[1]: "#ff7f0e",
    segments[2]: "#2ca02c",
}

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.08,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Total Sales by Segment", "Monthly Profit Margin by Segment"),
)

for seg in segments:
    seg_df = monthly[monthly["segment"] == seg].sort_values("month")
    color = colors.get(seg, None)

    # Top panel: grouped bar — monthly sales
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["total_sales"],
            name=str(seg),
            marker_color=color,
            legendgroup=str(seg),
            showlegend=True,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

    # Bottom panel: line — monthly profit margin
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["profit_margin"],
            name=str(seg),
            mode="lines+markers",
            line=dict(color=color, width=2),
            marker=dict(size=5),
            legendgroup=str(seg),
            showlegend=False,
        ),
        row=2,
        col=1,
    )

fig.update_layout(
    title=dict(text="Sales & Profit Margin KPI Dashboard", font=dict(size=18)),
    barmode="group",
    legend=dict(title="Segment", orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, xanchor="right", x=1),
    height=650,
    plot_bgcolor="white",
    paper_bgcolor="white",
)

fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Total Sales ($)", row=1, col=1, gridcolor="lightgrey")
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Profit Margin", tickformat=".1%", row=2, col=1, gridcolor="lightgrey")
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=2, col=1)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

User request:
Build a two-panel subplot figure for financial KPI exploration. The top panel should show monthly total sales as a grouped bar chart broken out by segment, and the bottom panel should show monthly profit margin (profit / sales) as a line chart per segment — both sharing the same x-axis so trends can be compared together.

Columns in df:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Monthly Sales & Profit Margin by Segment")

# Derive year-month period for grouping
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()

# Aggregate sales and profit by month and segment
agg = (
    df.groupby(["month", "segment"], observed=True)
    .agg(total_sales=("sales", "sum"), total_profit=("profit", "sum"))
    .reset_index()
)
agg["profit_margin"] = agg["total_profit"] / agg["total_sales"]

segments = agg["segment"].unique()

# Color palette per segment
palette = ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd"]
color_map = {seg: palette[i % len(palette)] for i, seg in enumerate(segments)}

# Build 2-row subplot sharing the x-axis
fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.08,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Total Sales by Segment", "Monthly Profit Margin by Segment"),
)

# --- Top panel: grouped bar chart of total sales per segment ---
for seg in segments:
    seg_data = agg[agg["segment"] == seg]
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_data["month"],
            y=seg_data["total_sales"],
            name=str(seg),
            marker_color=color_map[seg],
            legendgroup=str(seg),
            showlegend=True,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

# --- Bottom panel: line chart of profit margin per segment ---
for seg in segments:
    seg_data = agg[agg["segment"] == seg]
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_data["month"],
            y=seg_data["profit_margin"],
            name=str(seg),
            mode="lines+markers",
            line=dict(color=color_map[seg], width=2),
            marker=dict(size=5),
            legendgroup=str(seg),
            showlegend=False,
        ),
        row=2,
        col=1,
    )

# Layout polish
fig.update_layout(
    barmode="group",
    height=650,
    legend=dict(title="Segment", orientation="h", y=-0.12, x=0.5, xanchor="center"),
    plot_bgcolor="white",
    paper_bgcolor="white",
    font=dict(size=12),
    margin=dict(t=60, b=60, l=60, r=20),
)

fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Sales ($)", row=1, col=1, tickformat="$,.0f", showgrid=True, gridcolor="lightgrey")
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Profit Margin", row=2, col=1, tickformat=".1%", showgrid=True, gridcolor="lightgrey")
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=2, col=1, showgrid=False)
fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=False, row=1, col=1)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()

        go.Box(
            x=df.loc[mask, "segment"],
            y=df.loc[mask, "profit"],
            name=region,
            marker_color=palette[i % len(palette)],
            boxpoints="outliers",
            legendgroup=region,
            showlegend=False
        ),
        row=1, col=2
    )

fig.update_layout(

import streamlit as st import plotly.graph_objects as go st.title("Monthly Executive Dashboard: Sales vs. Profit by Segment") # Aggregate: sum sales and profit per segment + month agg = df.groupby(["segment", "month"], observed=True).agg( total_sales=("sales", "sum"), total_profit=("profit", "sum") ).reset_index() # Normalize marker size relative to max sales for visual scaling max_sales = agg["total_sales"].max() min_size, max_size = 8, 40 segment_colors = { seg: color for seg, color in zip( agg["segment"].unique(), ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd"] ) } fig = go.Figure() for seg in agg["segment"].unique(): seg_df = agg[agg["segment"] == seg].copy() scaled_size = ( min_size + (seg_df["total_sales"] / max_sales) * (max_size - min_size) ) fig.add_trace( go.Scatter( x=seg_df["total_sales"], y=seg_df["total_profit"], mode="markers", name=str(seg), marker=dict( size=scaled_size, color=segment_colors.get(seg, "#7f7f7f"), opacity=0.80, line=dict(width=1, color="white"), ), text=seg_df["month"], hovertemplate=( "%{text}

User request:
Build a two-panel subplot figure for financial KPI exploration. The top panel should show monthly total sales as a bar chart, with a separate trace per segment. The bottom panel should show monthly profit margin (profit / sales) as a line chart, also broken out by segment. Share the x-axis between both panels and add meaningful axis labels and a dashboard title.

Columns in df:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.title("Financial KPI Explorer: Sales & Profit Margin by Segment")

# Aggregate monthly metrics per segment
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()
monthly = (
    df.groupby(["month", "segment"], observed=True)
    .agg(total_sales=("sales", "sum"), total_profit=("profit", "sum"))
    .reset_index()
)
monthly["profit_margin"] = monthly["total_profit"] / monthly["total_sales"]

segments = monthly["segment"].unique()

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.10,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Total Sales by Segment", "Monthly Profit Margin by Segment"),
)

colors = ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd"]

for i, seg in enumerate(segments):
    seg_df = monthly[monthly["segment"] == seg].sort_values("month")
    color = colors[i % len(colors)]

    # Top panel: stacked bar of total sales
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["total_sales"],
            name=str(seg),
            marker_color=color,
            legendgroup=str(seg),
            showlegend=True,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

    # Bottom panel: line of profit margin
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["profit_margin"],
            name=str(seg),
            mode="lines+markers",
            line=dict(color=color, width=2),
            marker=dict(size=5),
            legendgroup=str(seg),
            showlegend=False,
        ),
        row=2,
        col=1,
    )

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="stack",
    height=620,
    legend=dict(title="Segment", orientation="v", x=1.02, y=1),
    hovermode="x unified",
    template="plotly_white",
    margin=dict(t=80, r=160),
)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Total Sales ($)", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Profit Margin", tickformat=".1%", row=2, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=2, col=1)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)
Generate a Streamlit app snippet using plotly.graph_objects only. Assume df already exists. Return code only.

    if i < len(segments)
}

fig = go.Figure()

for seg in segments:

        go.Box(
            x=seg_df["region"],

    ),
    legend=dict(

        y=1.02,
        xanchor="right",

)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.title("Sales & Profit Distribution by Segment and Region")

segments = df["segment"].unique()
regions = df["region"].unique()

color_map = {
    "East": "#1f77b4",
    "West": "#ff7f0e",
    "Central": "#2ca02c",
    "South": "#d62728",
}

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=1,
    cols=2,
    subplot_titles=("Sales Distribution by Segment & Region", "Profit Distribution by Segment & Region"),
    shared_yaxes=False,
)

added_to_legend = set()

for region in regions:
    region_df = df[df["region"] == region]
    show_legend = region not in added_to_legend
    added_to_legend.add(region)

    fig.add_trace(
        go.Box(
            x=region_df["segment"],
            y=region_df["sales"],
            name=str(region),
            marker_color=color_map.get(str(region), None),
            legendgroup=str(region),
            showlegend=show_legend,
            boxmean="sd",
            boxpoints="outliers",
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

    fig.add_trace(
        go.Box(
            x=region_df["segment"],
            y=region_df["profit"],
            name=str(region),
            marker_color=color_map.get(str(region), None),
            legendgroup=str(region),
            showlegend=False,
            boxmean="sd",
            boxpoints="outliers",
        ),
        row=1,
        col=2,
    )

fig.update_layout(
    boxmode="group",
    height=550,
    legend_title_text="Region",
    title_text="Operations Trend Analysis: Sales & Profit Spread by Segment",
    yaxis=dict(title="Sales ($)"),
    yaxis2=dict(title="Profit ($)"),
    xaxis=dict(title="Segment"),
    xaxis2=dict(title="Segment"),
    template="plotly_white",
)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

User request:
Build a two-panel subplot for financial KPI exploration: the top panel should show monthly total sales and profit as grouped bars by segment, and the bottom panel should show the profit margin (profit / sales) trend over time as a line chart per segment.

Columns in df:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Financial KPI Explorer: Sales, Profit & Margin by Segment")

# Aggregate monthly totals by segment
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()
monthly = df.groupby(["month", "segment"], observed=True).agg(
    total_sales=("sales", "sum"),
    total_profit=("profit", "sum")
).reset_index()
monthly["margin"] = monthly["total_profit"] / monthly["total_sales"]

segments = monthly["segment"].unique()

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.12,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Sales & Profit by Segment", "Profit Margin % by Segment Over Time")
)

colors_sales = ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728"]
colors_margin = ["#9467bd", "#8c564b", "#e377c2", "#7f7f7f"]

for i, seg in enumerate(segments):
    seg_df = monthly[monthly["segment"] == seg].sort_values("month")
    color_s = colors_sales[i % len(colors_sales)]
    color_m = colors_margin[i % len(colors_margin)]

    # Grouped bars for sales
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["total_sales"],
            name=f"{seg} — Sales",
            marker_color=color_s,
            opacity=0.85,
            legendgroup=str(seg),
            offsetgroup=str(seg),
        ),
        row=1, col=1
    )

    # Grouped bars for profit (same offsetgroup to place side-by-side)
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["total_profit"],
            name=f"{seg} — Profit",
            marker_color=color_s,
            marker_pattern_shape="/",
            opacity=0.55,
            legendgroup=str(seg),
            offsetgroup=f"{seg}_profit",
        ),
        row=1, col=1
    )

    # Margin line
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=(seg_df["margin"] * 100).round(2),
            mode="lines+markers",
            name=f"{seg} — Margin %",
            line=dict(color=color_m, width=2),
            marker=dict(size=5),
            legendgroup=str(seg),
        ),
        row=2, col=1
    )

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="group",
    height=650,
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=-0.25, xanchor="center", x=0.5),
    plot_bgcolor="white",
    paper_bgcolor="white",
    font=dict(size=12),

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Financial KPI Explorer — Sales & Profit by Segment")

# Derive year-month period for aggregation
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").astype(str)

# Aggregate sales and profit by month and segment
agg = (
    df.groupby(["month", "segment"], as_index=False)
    .agg(sales=("sales", "sum"), profit=("profit", "sum"))
    .sort_values("month")
)

segments = agg["segment"].unique().tolist()
colors = ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c"]

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=1,
    cols=2,
    shared_xaxes=False,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Sales by Segment", "Monthly Profit by Segment"),
    horizontal_spacing=0.12,
)

for i, seg in enumerate(segments):
    seg_df = agg[agg["segment"] == seg]
    color = colors[i % len(colors)]

    # Left panel — grouped bar chart for sales
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["sales"],
            name=seg,
            marker_color=color,
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=True,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

    # Right panel — line chart for profit
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["profit"],
            mode="lines+markers",
            name=seg,
            line=dict(color=color, width=2),
            marker=dict(size=5),
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=False,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=2,
    )

fig.update_layout(
    title_text="Monthly Sales & Profit KPIs by Customer Segment",
    title_font_size=16,
    barmode="group",
    legend_title_text="Segment",
    height=480,
    template="plotly_white",
)

fig.update_xaxes(tickangle=45, tickfont_size=10)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Total Sales ($)", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Total Profit ($)", row=1, col=2)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

Create a two-panel subplot figure for financial KPI exploration: the top panel should show monthly total sales as a bar chart with each segment as a separate stacked bar trace, and the bottom panel should show monthly profit margin (profit / sales) as a line chart. Both panels should share the x-axis.

Dataframe columns:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Monthly Sales by Segment & Profit Margin")

# Derive year-month period for grouping
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").astype(str)

# Aggregate monthly totals
monthly_totals = df.groupby("month")[["sales", "profit"]].sum().reset_index()
monthly_totals["profit_margin"] = monthly_totals["profit"] / monthly_totals["sales"]

# Aggregate monthly sales by segment
monthly_segment = (
    df.groupby(["month", "segment"])["sales"]
    .sum()
    .reset_index()
)

segments = monthly_segment["segment"].unique()
months = monthly_totals["month"].tolist()

# Build subplot: row 1 = stacked bar (sales by segment), row 2 = line (profit margin)
fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.08,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Sales by Segment", "Monthly Profit Margin (%)"),
)

colors = ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd"]

for i, seg in enumerate(segments):
    seg_data = monthly_segment[monthly_segment["segment"] == seg]
    seg_data = seg_data.set_index("month").reindex(months, fill_value=0).reset_index()
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_data["month"],
            y=seg_data["sales"],
            name=str(seg),
            marker_color=colors[i % len(colors)],
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=monthly_totals["month"],
        y=(monthly_totals["profit_margin"] * 100).round(2),
        mode="lines+markers",
        name="Profit Margin %",
        line=dict(color="#e377c2", width=2),
        marker=dict(size=6),
    ),
    row=2,
    col=1,
)

    legend=dict(

User request:
Create a two-panel subplot figure for financial KPI exploration. The top panel should show monthly total sales as a bar chart with one bar series per segment (Consumer, Corporate, Home Office) stacked on top of each other. The bottom panel should show the monthly profit margin (profit / sales) as a line chart, one line per segment. Both panels should share the x-axis. Add a descriptive title and axis labels.

Dataframe columns:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Monthly Sales & Profit Margin by Segment")

# Derive year-month period for grouping
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()

segments = df["segment"].unique()

# Aggregate monthly sales and profit per segment
monthly = (
    df.groupby(["month", "segment"], observed=True)
    .agg(total_sales=("sales", "sum"), total_profit=("profit", "sum"))
    .reset_index()
)
monthly["profit_margin"] = monthly["total_profit"] / monthly["total_sales"]

# Build subplot: 2 rows, 1 col, shared x-axis
fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.10,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Sales by Segment (Stacked)", "Monthly Profit Margin by Segment"),
)

colors = {"Consumer": "#1f77b4", "Corporate": "#ff7f0e", "Home Office": "#2ca02c"}

for seg in segments:
    seg_data = monthly[monthly["segment"] == seg].sort_values("month")
    color = colors.get(seg, None)

    # Top panel: stacked bar for total sales
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_data["month"],
            y=seg_data["total_sales"],
            name=seg,
            marker_color=color,
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=True,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

    # Bottom panel: line for profit margin
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_data["month"],
            y=seg_data["profit_margin"],
            mode="lines+markers",
            name=seg,
            line=dict(color=color, width=2),
            marker=dict(size=5),
            legendgroup=seg,
            showlegend=False,
        ),
        row=2,
        col=1,
    )

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="stack",
    height=650,
    title_text="Financial KPI Explorer — Sales & Profit Margin by Segment",
    title_font_size=16,
    legend=dict(title="Segment", orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, xanchor="right", x=1),
    plot_bgcolor="white",
    paper_bgcolor="white",
)

fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Total Sales ($)", row=1, col=1, tickprefix="$", separatethousands=True)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Profit Margin", row=2, col=1, tickformat=".1%")
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=2, col=1)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.title("Sales & Profit Distribution by Segment and Region")

regions = df["region"].dropna().unique()
segments = df["segment"].dropna().unique()

color_map = {
    "Consumer": "#1f77b4",
    "Corporate": "#ff7f0e",
    "Home Office": "#2ca02c",
}

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=1,
    cols=2,
    subplot_titles=("Sales Distribution by Segment & Region", "Profit Distribution by Segment & Region"),
    shared_yaxes=False,
)

for i, segment in enumerate(segments):
    seg_df = df[df["segment"] == segment]
    color = list(color_map.values())[i % len(color_map)]

    fig.add_trace(
        go.Box(
            x=seg_df["region"],
            y=seg_df["sales"],
            name=segment,
            marker_color=color,
            boxmean="sd",
            legendgroup=segment,
            showlegend=True,
            boxpoints="outliers",
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

    fig.add_trace(
        go.Box(
            x=seg_df["region"],
            y=seg_df["profit"],
            name=segment,
            marker_color=color,
            boxmean="sd",
            legendgroup=segment,
            showlegend=False,
            boxpoints="outliers",
        ),
        row=1,
        col=2,
    )

fig.update_layout(
    boxmode="group",
    title=dict(
        text="Operations Trend Analysis: Sales & Profit Distributions by Segment and Region",
        x=0.5,
        xanchor="center",
    ),
    legend=dict(title="Segment", orientation="v"),
    template="plotly_white",
    height=520,
)

fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Sales ($)", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Profit ($)", row=1, col=2)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Region", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Region", row=1, col=2)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.title("Marketing Performance Summary: Customer & Profit Distributions")

segments = df["segment"].unique()
segment_colors = {}
palette = ["#636EFA", "#EF553B", "#00CC96", "#AB63FA"]
for i, seg in enumerate(segments):
    segment_colors[seg] = palette[i % len(palette)]

regions = df["region"].unique()
region_colors = {}
region_palette = ["#FFA15A", "#19D3F3", "#FF6692", "#B6E880"]
for i, reg in enumerate(regions):
    region_colors[reg] = region_palette[i % len(region_palette)]

df["profit_margin"] = df["profit"] / df["sales"].replace(0, float("nan"))

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=1,
    cols=2,
    subplot_titles=(
        "Customer Age Distribution by Segment",
        "Profit Margin Distribution by Region",
    ),
    horizontal_spacing=0.12,
)

for seg in segments:
    subset = df[df["segment"] == seg]
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Histogram(
            x=subset["customer_age"],
            name=str(seg),
            nbinsx=20,
            opacity=0.65,
            marker=dict(color=segment_colors[seg]),
            legendgroup=str(seg),
            legendgrouptitle_text="Segment" if seg == segments[0] else "",
            bingroup=1,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

for reg in regions:
    subset = df[df["region"] == reg]
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Histogram(
            x=subset["profit_margin"],
            name=str(reg),
            nbinsx=20,
            opacity=0.65,
            marker=dict(color=region_colors[reg]),
            legendgroup=str(reg),
            legendgrouptitle_text="Region" if reg == regions[0] else "",
            bingroup=2,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=2,
    )

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="overlay",
    height=480,
    title_text="Marketing Performance Summary — Customer Age & Profit Margin Distributions",
    title_x=0.5,
    legend=dict(groupclick="toggleitem", tracegroupgap=12),
    template="plotly_white",
    bargap=0.05,
)

fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Customer Age", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Count", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Profit Margin", row=1, col=2)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Count", row=1, col=2)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.title("Sales & Profit Distribution by Segment and Region")

segments = df["segment"].unique()
regions = df["region"].unique()

color_map = {
    "East": "#1f77b4",
    "West": "#ff7f0e",
    "Central": "#2ca02c",
    "South": "#d62728",
}

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=1,
    cols=2,
    subplot_titles=("Sales Distribution by Segment & Region", "Profit Distribution by Segment & Region"),
    shared_yaxes=False,
)

for i, region in enumerate(sorted(regions)):
    region_df = df[df["region"] == region]
    show_legend = True

    fig.add_trace(
        go.Box(
            x=region_df["segment"],
            y=region_df["sales"],
            name=region,
            marker_color=color_map.get(region, None),
            boxmean=True,
            legendgroup=region,
            showlegend=show_legend,
            boxpoints="outliers",
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

    fig.add_trace(
        go.Box(
            x=region_df["segment"],
            y=region_df["profit"],
            name=region,
            marker_color=color_map.get(region, None),
            boxmean=True,
            legendgroup=region,
            showlegend=False,
            boxpoints="outliers",
        ),
        row=1,
        col=2,
    )

fig.update_layout(
    boxmode="group",
    height=550,
    legend_title_text="Region",
    title_text="Operations Trend Analysis: Sales & Profit Spread by Segment",
    yaxis=dict(title="Sales ($)"),
    yaxis2=dict(title="Profit ($)"),
    template="plotly_white",
)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.title("Sales & Profit Distribution by Segment and Region")
st.markdown("Compare sales and profit spread across customer segments, broken down by region.")

segments = df["segment"].unique()
regions = df["region"].unique()

palette = {
    "East": "#1f77b4",
    "West": "#ff7f0e",
    "Central": "#2ca02c",
    "South": "#d62728",
}

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=1, cols=2,
    subplot_titles=("Sales Distribution by Segment & Region", "Profit Distribution by Segment & Region"),
    shared_yaxes=False
)

shown_in_legend = set()

for region in regions:
    region_df = df[df["region"] == region]
    color = palette.get(str(region), "#7f7f7f")
    show_legend = region not in shown_in_legend
    shown_in_legend.add(region)

    fig.add_trace(
        go.Box(
            x=region_df["segment"],
            y=region_df["sales"],
            name=str(region),
            marker_color=color,
            boxmean="sd",
            legendgroup=str(region),
            showlegend=show_legend,
            boxpoints="outliers",
        ),
        row=1, col=1
    )

    fig.add_trace(
        go.Box(
            x=region_df["segment"],
            y=region_df["profit"],
            name=str(region),
            marker_color=color,
            boxmean="sd",
            legendgroup=str(region),
            showlegend=False,
            boxpoints="outliers",
        ),
        row=1, col=2
    )

fig.update_layout(
    boxmode="group",
    height=520,
    legend_title_text="Region",
    title_text="Operations Trend Analysis: Sales & Profit Distributions",
    title_font_size=16,
    yaxis=dict(title="Sales ($)"),
    yaxis2=dict(title="Profit ($)"),
    xaxis=dict(title="Customer Segment"),
    xaxis2=dict(title="Customer Segment"),
    plot_bgcolor="#f9f9f9",
)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

Build a two-panel subplot figure for financial KPI exploration: the top panel should show monthly total sales and profit as grouped bars (one trace per metric, grouped by month), and the bottom panel should show the month-over-month profit margin percentage as a line chart. import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Monthly Financial KPI Explorer")

# Aggregate by month
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").astype(str)
monthly = df.groupby("month", as_index=False).agg(total_sales=("sales", "sum"), total_profit=("profit", "sum"))
monthly = monthly.sort_values("month")
monthly["profit_margin"] = (monthly["total_profit"] / monthly["total_sales"] * 100).round(2)

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.12,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Sales & Profit", "Profit Margin (%)")
)

# Top panel: grouped bars for sales and profit
fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["total_sales"],
        name="Total Sales",
        marker_color="steelblue",
        offsetgroup=0
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)
fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["total_profit"],
        name="Total Profit",
        marker_color="mediumseagreen",
        offsetgroup=1
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

# Bottom panel: profit margin line
fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["profit_margin"],
        name="Profit Margin %",
        mode="lines+markers",
        line=dict(color="tomato", width=2),
        marker=dict(size=6)
    ),
    row=2, col=1
)

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="group",
    height=600,
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, xanchor="right", x=1),
    plot_bgcolor="white",
    title_text="Financial KPI Dashboard – Monthly Overview",
    title_font_size=16
)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Amount ($)", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Margin (%)", row=2, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=2, col=1, tickangle=45)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

st.title("Product Performance Review — Revenue & Sales by Segment")

# Aggregate monthly revenue and sales per segment
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()
monthly = df.groupby(["month", "segment"], observed=True)[["revenue", "sales"]].sum().reset_index()

segments = monthly["segment"].unique()

color_map = [
    "#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728",
    "#9467bd", "#8c564b", "#e377c2", "#7f7f7f"
]

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.10,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Revenue by Segment", "Monthly Sales Volume by Segment"),
    row_heights=[0.55, 0.45],
)

for i, seg in enumerate(segments):
    seg_df = monthly[monthly["segment"] == seg].sort_values("month")
    color = color_map[i % len(color_map)]

    # Top subplot — revenue trend
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["revenue"],
            mode="lines+markers",
            name=str(seg),
            line=dict(color=color, width=2),
            marker=dict(size=5),
            legendgroup=str(seg),
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

    # Bottom subplot — sales volume trend
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["sales"],
            mode="lines",
            name=str(seg),
            line=dict(color=color, width=2, dash="dot"),
            legendgroup=str(seg),
            showlegend=False,
        ),
        row=2,
        col=1,
    )

fig.update_layout(
    height=650,
    legend=dict(
        title=dict(text="Segment"),
        orientation="v",
        x=1.02,
        y=1.0,
    ),
    hovermode="x unified",
    template="plotly_white",
    margin=dict(l=60, r=150, t=60, b=50),
)

fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Revenue ($)", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Sales Volume", row=2, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=2, col=1)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

User request:
Build a two-panel subplot figure for financial KPI exploration. The top panel should show monthly total sales as a bar chart with one bar series per customer segment (Consumer, Corporate, Home Office), stacked. The bottom panel should show the monthly profit margin (profit / sales) as a line chart, one line per segment. Share the x-axis between both panels and add clear axis and subplot titles.

Columns in df:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Monthly Sales & Profit Margin by Segment")

# Derive year-month period for aggregation
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()

segments = df["segment"].unique()

# Aggregate sales and profit by month and segment
monthly = (
    df.groupby(["month", "segment"], observed=True)
    .agg(total_sales=("sales", "sum"), total_profit=("profit", "sum"))
    .reset_index()
)
monthly["profit_margin"] = monthly["total_profit"] / monthly["total_sales"]

# Color palette per segment
palette = {
    "Consumer": "#1f77b4",
    "Corporate": "#ff7f0e",
    "Home Office": "#2ca02c",
}

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.12,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Total Sales by Segment", "Monthly Profit Margin by Segment"),
)

for seg in segments:
    seg_data = monthly[monthly["segment"] == seg].sort_values("month")
    color = palette.get(str(seg), None)

    # Top panel: stacked bar for total sales
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_data["month"],
            y=seg_data["total_sales"],
            name=str(seg),
            marker_color=color,
            legendgroup=str(seg),
            showlegend=True,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

    # Bottom panel: line chart for profit margin
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_data["month"],
            y=seg_data["profit_margin"],
            mode="lines+markers",
            name=str(seg),
            line=dict(color=color, width=2),
            marker=dict(size=5),
            legendgroup=str(seg),
            showlegend=False,
        ),
        row=2,
        col=1,
    )

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="stack",
    height=650,
    legend=dict(title="Segment", orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.04, xanchor="right", x=1),
    plot_bgcolor="white",
    paper_bgcolor="white",
)

fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Sales ($)", row=1, col=1, tickformat="$,.0f", showgrid=True, gridcolor="lightgrey")
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Profit Margin", row=2, col=1, tickformat=".1%", showgrid=True, gridcolor="lightgrey")
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=2, col=1)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Financial KPI Explorer: Monthly Sales, Profit & Margin")

# Aggregate to monthly granularity
df["month"] = pd.to_datetime(df["date"]).dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()
monthly = df.groupby("month", as_index=False).agg(sales=("sales", "sum"), profit=("profit", "sum"))
monthly["profit_margin"] = (monthly["profit"] / monthly["sales"] * 100).round(2)

# Build 2-row subplot with shared x-axis
fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2, cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.12,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Sales vs. Profit", "Profit Margin (%)")
)

# Row 1 — grouped bar: Sales
fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["sales"],
        name="Sales",
        marker_color="steelblue",
        offsetgroup=0
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

# Row 1 — grouped bar: Profit
fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["profit"],
        name="Profit",
        marker_color="mediumseagreen",
        offsetgroup=1
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

# Row 2 — line chart: Profit Margin %
fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["profit_margin"],
        name="Profit Margin %",
        mode="lines+markers",
        line=dict(color="tomato", width=2),
        marker=dict(size=5)
    ),
    row=2, col=1
)

fig.update_layout(
    height=600,
    barmode="group",
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, xanchor="right", x=1),
    plot_bgcolor="white",
    title_text="Monthly Financial KPI Dashboard",
    title_font_size=16
)

fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Amount ($)", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Margin (%)", row=2, col=1)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=2, col=1)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go

st.title("Profit Distribution by Segment & Product — Operations Trend Analysis")

# Compute profit margin per row
df = df.copy()
df["profit_margin"] = df["profit"] / df["sales"].replace(0, float("nan")) * 100

segments = df["segment"].dropna().unique()
products = df["product"].dropna().unique()

colors = {
    "Consumer": "#1f77b4",
    "Corporate": "#ff7f0e",
    "Home Office": "#2ca02c",
}

fig = go.Figure()

for seg in segments:
    seg_df = df[df["segment"] == seg]
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Box(
            x=seg_df["product"],
            y=seg_df["profit_margin"],
            name=seg,
            marker_color=colors.get(seg, None),
            boxmean="sd",
            boxpoints="outliers",
            jitter=0.3,
            whiskerwidth=0.6,
            legendgroup=seg,
        )
    )

fig.update_layout(
    boxmode="group",
    title=dict(
        text="Profit Margin Distribution by Segment and Product Category",
        font=dict(size=16),
    ),
    xaxis=dict(
        title="Product Category",
        tickfont=dict(size=12),
    ),
    yaxis=dict(
        title="Profit Margin (%)",
        zeroline=True,
        zerolinecolor="lightgray",
        gridcolor="lightgray",
    ),
    legend=dict(
        title=dict(text="Segment"),
        orientation="h",
        yanchor="bottom",
        y=1.02,
        xanchor="right",
        x=1,
    ),
    plot_bgcolor="white",
    height=550,
)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Financial KPI Explorer: Trends & Segment Breakdown")

# --- Aggregations ---
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()
monthly = df.groupby("month", as_index=False)[["sales", "profit"]].sum()

segment_summary = df.groupby("segment", as_index=False)[["sales", "profit"]].sum()

# --- Subplot layout: 1 row, 2 cols ---
fig = make_subplots(
    rows=1,
    cols=2,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Sales & Profit Trend", "Sales & Profit by Segment"),
    horizontal_spacing=0.12
)

# --- Left: Multi-line time series ---
fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["sales"],
        mode="lines+markers",
        name="Monthly Sales",
        line=dict(color="#1f77b4", width=2),
        marker=dict(size=5)
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["profit"],
        mode="lines+markers",
        name="Monthly Profit",
        line=dict(color="#2ca02c", width=2, dash="dot"),
        marker=dict(size=5)
    ),
    row=1, col=1
)

# --- Right: Grouped bar by segment ---
fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=segment_summary["segment"],
        y=segment_summary["sales"],
        name="Segment Sales",
        marker_color="#1f77b4",
        offsetgroup=0
    ),
    row=1, col=2
)

fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=segment_summary["segment"],
        y=segment_summary["profit"],
        name="Segment Profit",
        marker_color="#2ca02c",
        offsetgroup=1
    ),
    row=1, col=2
)

# --- Layout ---
fig.update_layout(
    height=480,
    barmode="group",
    title_text="Financial KPI Dashboard",
    title_font=dict(size=18),
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=-0.25, xanchor="center", x=0.5),
    plot_bgcolor="#f9f9f9",
    paper_bgcolor="white",
    font=dict(size=12)
)

fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=1, col=1, tickangle=-30)
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Segment", row=1, col=2)

User request:
Create a two-panel subplot figure for financial KPI exploration — the top panel shows monthly aggregated sales as a bar chart with one trace per segment, and the bottom panel shows monthly profit margin (profit / sales) as a line chart per segment, with a shared x-axis so both panels align by month.

Dataframe columns:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Financial KPI Explorer: Sales & Profit Margin by Segment")

# Derive year-month period for aggregation
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()

segments = df["segment"].unique()

# Aggregate sales and profit by month and segment
agg = (
    df.groupby(["month", "segment"], observed=True)
    .agg(sales=("sales", "sum"), profit=("profit", "sum"))
    .reset_index()
)
agg["profit_margin"] = agg["profit"] / agg["sales"]

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.10,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Sales by Segment", "Monthly Profit Margin by Segment"),
)

colors = ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd"]

for i, seg in enumerate(segments):
    seg_df = agg[agg["segment"] == seg].sort_values("month")
    color = colors[i % len(colors)]

    # Top panel: stacked bar of sales
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["sales"],
            name=str(seg),
            marker_color=color,
            legendgroup=str(seg),
            showlegend=True,
        ),
        row=1,
        col=1,
    )

    # Bottom panel: line of profit margin
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["profit_margin"],
            name=str(seg),
            mode="lines+markers",
            line=dict(color=color, width=2),
            marker=dict(size=5),
            legendgroup=str(seg),
            showlegend=False,
        ),
        row=2,
        col=1,
    )

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="stack",
    height=620,
    legend_title_text="Segment",
    title_text="Sales & Profit Margin KPI Dashboard",
    title_font_size=16,
    hovermode="x unified",
)

fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Total Sales ($)", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Profit Margin", tickformat=".1%", row=2, col=1)

User request:
Create a two-panel subplot dashboard for financial KPI exploration. The top panel should show monthly total sales and profit as grouped bars per segment (side-by-side), and the bottom panel should show the profit margin (profit / sales) trend over time as a line chart per segment. Use shared x-axis and add axis labels and a dashboard title.

Dataframe columns:
date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Financial KPI Explorer: Sales, Profit & Margin by Segment")

# Aggregate monthly metrics per segment
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").dt.to_timestamp()
monthly = df.groupby(["month", "segment"], observed=True).agg(
    sales=("sales", "sum"),
    profit=("profit", "sum")
).reset_index()
monthly["margin"] = monthly["profit"] / monthly["sales"]

segments = monthly["segment"].unique()

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=True,
    vertical_spacing=0.12,
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Sales & Profit by Segment", "Profit Margin (%) by Segment Over Time")
)

colors_sales = ["#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728"]
colors_margin = ["#9467bd", "#8c564b", "#e377c2", "#7f7f7f"]

for i, seg in enumerate(segments):
    seg_df = monthly[monthly["segment"] == seg].sort_values("month")
    color = colors_sales[i % len(colors_sales)]

    # Top panel: grouped bars for sales
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["sales"],
            name=f"{seg} Sales",
            marker_color=color,
            legendgroup=seg,
            offsetgroup=f"{seg}_sales"
        ),
        row=1, col=1
    )

    # Top panel: grouped bars for profit
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["profit"],
            name=f"{seg} Profit",
            marker_color=color,
            opacity=0.5,
            legendgroup=seg,
            offsetgroup=f"{seg}_profit"
        ),
        row=1, col=1
    )

    # Bottom panel: profit margin line per segment
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=seg_df["month"],
            y=seg_df["margin"] * 100,
            mode="lines+markers",
            name=f"{seg} Margin",
            line=dict(color=colors_margin[i % len(colors_margin)], width=2),
            marker=dict(size=5),
            legendgroup=seg
        ),
        row=2, col=1
    )

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="group",
    height=700,
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=-0.25, xanchor="center", x=0.5),
    plot_bgcolor="white",
    paper_bgcolor="white"
)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Amount ($)", row=1, col=1, showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eeeeee")
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Margin (%)", row=2, col=1, showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eeeeee")
fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Month", row=2, col=1)

st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True)

Create a two-panel subplot dashboard for financial KPI exploration — the top panel shows monthly total sales as a bar chart with a profit margin line overlay (dual y-axis), and the bottom panel shows a grouped bar chart comparing total sales across segments for each region.

date: datetime
sales: float
profit: float
region: category
segment: category
product: category

import streamlit as st
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
import pandas as pd

st.title("Financial KPI Explorer")

# --- Top panel: monthly sales bar + profit margin line (dual y-axis) ---
df["month"] = df["date"].dt.to_period("M").astype(str)
monthly = df.groupby("month", sort=True).agg(total_sales=("sales", "sum"), total_profit=("profit", "sum")).reset_index()
monthly["profit_margin"] = monthly["total_profit"] / monthly["total_sales"] * 100

# --- Bottom panel: segment x region grouped bar ---
seg_region = df.groupby(["region", "segment"], sort=True).agg(total_sales=("sales", "sum")).reset_index()
regions = seg_region["region"].unique()
segments = seg_region["segment"].unique()

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2, cols=1,
    shared_xaxes=False,
    vertical_spacing=0.18,
    specs=[[{"secondary_y": True}], [{"secondary_y": False}]],
    subplot_titles=("Monthly Sales & Profit Margin", "Sales by Segment and Region")
)

# Top panel — sales bars
fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["total_sales"],
        name="Total Sales",
        marker_color="steelblue",
        opacity=0.8
    ),
    row=1, col=1, secondary_y=False
)

# Top panel — profit margin line
fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=monthly["month"],
        y=monthly["profit_margin"],
        name="Profit Margin %",
        mode="lines+markers",
        line=dict(color="tomato", width=2),
        marker=dict(size=6)
    ),
    row=1, col=1, secondary_y=True
)

# Bottom panel — grouped bars per segment, one trace per segment

}

for seg in segments:

    fig.add_trace(
        go.Box(

        )
    )

    title=dict(

        font=dict(size=18),
    ),
    xaxis=dict(
        title="Product",
        tickangle=-30,
    ),
    yaxis=dict(
        title="Profit ($)",
        zeroline=True,
        zerolinecolor="lightgrey",
        gridcolor="#eeeeee",
    ),
    legend=dict(
        title="Segment",
        orientation="h",
        yanchor="bottom",
        y=1.02,
        xanchor="right",

)
